messages.message: My dear friend, I must commend your position in southern Europe—it’s a beautiful front. I have no interest in conflict with you, and I believe our mutual goal is to keep Austria and Germany from gaining too much strength. I’m planning to hold my core centers and maybe even support your ambitions if we ever need to coordinate. Let’s keep each other updated!
Hi Italy! The standard IA is always in the way, so I'm happy to go another route if you're interested.

I admire your strength in the north and your balance of armies and fleets. I see no reason for conflict between us. I intend to consolidate my supply centers and may even support your goals if needed. Let’s keep the peace and focus on the real threat—Germany’s ambitions!

Hi France,
Just reaching out to establish comms and establish how we might be able to work together in the long run. What would you say to a DMZ in Burgundy?
Hi Germany! Can I interest you in the traditional Anschluss?
Hey Russia, how do you feel about the R/T? I reckon we could work quite well together!

Hello Austria,
I'd love to get on your good side, as Austria and Germany need to stick together. What's your plan for opening?

Hi England,
Just getting in touch to establish comms and figure out how you see the game. Do you have any interest in working together?

Hi Italy,
I'd like to stay in touch and figure out what you see happening in the future. We share neighbors in Austria and France so we might have some common interests. What's your plan for the opening?

Hi Turkey,
We share neighbors in Austria and Russia, so it could be in both of our best interests to keep in touch and see if there are ways we can work together. What's your plan for the opening?

Hi Russia,
We share a lot of common interests. Do you see us getting along well? What's your plan for the opening?

Hey Italy, good luck this game!
I have no interest in getting dragged into the Med conflict either. I think us cooperating together in both the short and long term would be quite useful to both of us. Shall we start with a DMZ in Pie, Gol, Wmed and Naf?
Hey Germany! I haven't quite decided yet to be honest. We should definitely stay in touch though.
Hey England, hope all goes well up there! I feel we should keep each other informed. How are your dealings going?

Not sure yet - still trying to hear from Russia and Turkey. I'll keep you posted.

I look forward to working with you, since we share two neighbors, and a successful Germany is a successful Austria.
Hey Turkey, R/T is my favourite alliance and if done properly it never fails to work. Is there anything in particular you'd like to start the game with? How would you like to deal with the black sea?

Hello Germany, good luck this game!
I would be very interested in making Burgundy a DMZ. I think both of us will have enough to deal with other than to get bogged down in a direct conflict. I'd like to propose that I take Belgium and you take Holland and Denmark. We could work together on pushing England out of Scandinavia and out of our backdoor. Thoughts?
Hi Russia! Want to work together this game?
Agreed. I haven't heard back from Russia yet. Will let you know when I do.

I have always found Turkey’s position intriguing. I see little overlap between our interests and believe we can coexist peacefully. If ever we need to coordinate against a common foe, I’d be honored to work with you. Let’s keep the lines open!

I'm certainly open to that. If you agree to build fleets with your 2 builds, I will support you into Belgium in the autumn.

Have you heard from England yet? I haven't.
Hey Austria, yes I'd be happy to work together this game. Do you have anything in particular you'd like to start with? How would you like to deal with Galicia?
Agreed. Do you mind telling me if you decide to go after Austria or Russia? I could help you out with either.
Hey Austria, how would you like to see the Balkans divided?

Hey Germany,

Yes we do. There isn't much that can be done to start in the east. I have no intention of heading into Prussia or silesia, would you be happy with my fleet occupying sweden?

I would love to start with a DMZ in Galicia, if that works for you. Let me know what you think.

I am open to anything with regard to Turkey, though I find it rather hard to kill him on my own - he's usually quite difficult to finish off. What would your preference be?

Dear Austria, I appreciate your openness to alternative routes—your flexibility is admirable. I have no interest in complicating our mutual borders, and I’m more than happy to avoid any conflict. My focus remains on securing my own centers and ensuring stability in southern Europe. If you ever need support, know I’m here.
Of course. I guess I should probably open to the Black Sea?
Yes, I don't have any problems with that.
Hey England, I hope we can get on well. Is there anything in particular you'd like to start this game with? I'd like to occupy sweden but I would be more than happy with you taking norway.
Hey France, good luck! I feel we should stay in touch. How are your talks going?
Yeah, Russia tells me he is opening to Sweden, so the only way for him to move Mos->Sev is if he's planning on opening Sev->Rum.

Hi Germany, good luck this game!
I'm very much down for an EG. It's one of my favorite alliances, and I find it usually works better in the long run than an EF.
If you're willing to work together against France, we could even try and go all in and make it a WT. What do you think?
Thank you.
I would be quite happy to start with a DMZ, no? We could bounce to maintain the subterfuge.

Hi Turkey! I agree, and I also think we should keep each other informed.
Not too much so far, the game started at a rather awkward time for me. I've only just logged on. Anyways, good luck in the south!

Germany, your interest is welcome! I see great potential in our shared interests, especially with Austria and France nearby. My plan is to hold my core centers firmly and expand cautiously. If we align against a third party, I’m sure we can accomplish great things. Let’s keep the conversation open.
That's intriguing, but I wouldn't want to commit against France right off the bat. Could you commit to making an anti-Russian opening?
Hey Italy! How are you doing? I hope you have a good game, and hope we can keep in touch :)
Sounds good to me. Have you heard from England yet?
I'd be happy to dmz galicia and I'm open to starting with a more active assault on turkey. What would you like to do with the situation in the balkans?

Hello England, good luck this game!
I'd like to propose that we declare the English Channel a DMZ. I think the best we can do for ourselves at this stage is to work together instead of against each other, so I'd like to work together. Thoughts?
Hey Austria, how are you doing? I hope you have a good game and let's keep in touch!
Thanks! And to you in the north!
Nothing too interesting out of Russia yet. Have you decided on an opening?

France, I couldn’t agree more about the Med being a hotbed of unnecessary conflict! A DMZ in Pie, Gol, Wmed, and Naf sounds like a brilliant idea—let’s make it happen. I’m fully committed to a peaceful and cooperative relationship. Let’s work together to keep our neighbors in check.

Hey Russia, I really hope we can work together too. It's very rare for an EF to work out, and I'm interested to see if it can work out.
I would also like to get Norway, but it might be a bit difficult. Germany seems to want to be friendly and fight France, so I could very much use my forces in that direction, but that would mean that you wouldn't get Sweden. Is there anything I can do to convince you to let me have it?
Okay thanks.
I look forward to working with you! We're both in tricky positions, but we can make it work.
You too, and thanks!
I could do that. But I could also commit two fleets to an anti-French opening if you commit to go all in on France. That might help to secure the three of us against him.
Hello Turkey, good luck to you as well. It's been going well so far, how are things on your end?
Have you heard much from England? I'm wondering if I should trust what he's telling me.
Have you heard from Germany yet? He's acting a little strange.

Dear England, what a pleasure to hear from you! I admire your naval strength and the way you command the seas. I have no interest in conflict with you, and I believe we can be great allies in keeping balance across Europe. I'm focusing on securing my southern centers and maintaining peace in the region. Let's keep this line open—perhaps someday we can coordinate!
I'm more worried about Russia at the moment. Are you planning on opening to the Norwegian and North Seas? I would be very happy if that were the case.
Of course. What are you thinking of opening with?

Hey France, thanks for the message! I definitely agree that the best thing for both of us would be to DMZ the channel. A bounce would not be particularly productive for either of us at this point.
I agree that it is in our best interests to work together too, and I hope that we can do so.
Do you think you'll open to Burgundy? If so, we could do something interesting to make an EF work.
England seems friendly and he has mentioned that he is happy to head towards france if he has your support. I'm not sure how much to trust his words though.

Yes, I have heard from England. They have made some friendly suggestions but didn't present any real proposals. I think they're going to push for both the Channel and the north sea, so I think if we can keep them out of both of those then we'll be in good shape to push them out of the continent entirely in the next few years.
I'm open to anything, honestly - what did you have in mind?
If you were interested in opening anti-Russia, we could give that a go.
Most people seem friendly.
That sounds great! I look forward to working with you. We can also share information, since we won't be running into each other anytime soon. Have you heard anything interesting?
He hasn't given me anything concrete, either, so not yet.
Hey italy, I hope we can work together this game. Do you have anything in particular you'd like to start with?
If you want we can go anti-Austria straight away?
Yeah, that's how things are on my end too. That makes it even more important that we stay in touch, especially since we have no reason to come into conflict with each other for a while
I'd love to split them up 50/50 - SER and GRE for me and RUM and BUL for you. What do you think?
That sounds good. Have you got Germany on side?
Interesting. He seemed to be hinting at a WT to me, which I didn't really expect from him.
A bounce might be better. I would be happy to start the game with a bounce.
What's he saying? He's been fairly short with me so far, but he seemed friendly enough.
Have you heard anything from Turkey?
I have him as far as I know, but I'll wait until I hear back from him to make sure.

Hmm. Good to know. He seemed more anti-French than anything, but then again, England's early-game strategy is to get France and Germany to fight, so I guess that's not surprising.

What's your take on Austria and Turkey?
I shall open to Black Sea.
He asked how I wanted to split up the Balkans, so I assume he's making a play against Russia.
I think that should be good. Could we have a dmz in galicia and a bounce there this turn?
Sounds good - I'm opening SER and GAL.
How are your relations with Russia?
BTW, I would be more than happy to bounce Russia in Sweden if that's what you want to do.
Cool cool.
Okay.
Yes, I think I will
Did you hear anything from Italy?
By the way, have you heard anything from France yet?

Germany, I've been thinking about our shared interests along Austria and France’s borders. I see great potential for cooperation, especially if we want to avoid unnecessary conflict. I'm holding my core firmly and planning to expand cautiously. If you're open to it, let’s explore ways to support each other in the future!
Yes, that sounds good.
I'm going for an anti-Russia opening.
Nothing much really. We seem to have a basic understanding that peace between us would be beneficial.

Austria, your thoughtful approach is inspiring—and I couldn’t agree more about the need for stability in southern Europe. I’m entirely open to supporting your ambitions if we ever find ourselves facing a common challenge. Let’s keep this dialogue strong and open!
Ok, thanks for letting me know. Turkey is playing it close to the vest right now, so we'll have to see what he does with the Black Sea.
He's interested in working together but hasn't offered any concrete plans
Awesome!
Not much so far. I've only gotten one message from him. I'll tell you when I get more.
He said he was going there, but I don't think he told me.

My dear England, what a kind offer to share information! I appreciate your vision for peaceful cooperation. I haven't heard much beyond the usual posturing from Austria and Germany, but I remain open to hearing your insights. If you ever notice any aggressive moves from our neighbors, I’d be honored to hear your thoughts!
Good to know, thanks.
Austria seems friendly and I think I'm going to have a reasonable arrangement with him. Turkey is proving less willing to communicate.
Same here. I'm also a little confused as to why he isn't talking.
I had a brief conversation with France earlier. Nothing committal, but we didn't decide anything either.
OK, that's good to hear.
Austria has told me that he is heading to the Balkans, so I assume that means he is friendly with you as well.
He has expressed an interest in working together but I don't think he has any concrete plans yet. Have you heard much from him?
I think France is going to play it cautiously as well. He seemed very interested in working together, but wanted to keep his intentions close to his vest.
Cool, glad to hear it!
How do you mean?

Russia, what a pleasure to hear from you! I'm entirely open to working together—after all, our goals seem to align perfectly in keeping Western Europe from gaining too much strength. Perhaps we could coordinate on a future move against Austria or Germany? I'm holding my centers firmly and planning to expand cautiously.
Same thing - not much else.

That sounds great!
And I will be sure to keep you updated, especially about France and Germany.
I'm glad you seem positive about the idea. I was thinking of moving against Turkey.

Turkey, your aggressive vision is invigorating! An anti-Austria alliance sounds like a brilliant idea—I admire your boldness. Let’s be clear: I have no interest in encroaching on your space, and I’m fully open to supporting your goals against Austria. Let’s keep this conversation alive!
I think he's just trying to play close to the vest - it's a pretty standard strategy for Germany.
He didn't want to commit to an alliance, but indicated he was interested in working together.
Ah that's too bad. A Western Triple usually seems like a terrible idea for France, but the more fleets he has the better it is for us so I guess we'll just have to see how it goes

Dear Austria, your cool response warms my heart! I truly believe we can be great neighbors if we keep our focus on peace and stability. I’ll be holding my own centers firmly and will avoid any moves that might upset our shared borders. If ever you need a quiet ally, know I’m here.
Great!
I am usually of the same opinion.

I think a northern triple could be fruitful. You could support me into sweden as a thank you and I could then support you into Denmark. You would take norway with a fleet and I would get sweden. That gives all three of us a build at the end of 1901. What do you think?
Do you plan on opening with 2 units north?

My dear England, your offer to keep me updated on France and Germany is most welcome! I rely on your keen eyes on the seas and the West. I'm holding my southern centers tightly, and I’d be honored to hear your insights on how we might work together to maintain balance in the region.
No I don't think so.
I agree entirely. I'm not a huge fan of playing the middle powers - so many neighbors - and IA makes sense to me.
Are you still interested in supporting me into Belgium this turn?
I think Russia will bounce you in Black Sea.
Interesting. If you're not going after Austria, then I assume you're going after Turkey, right?
Hmm, that's a shame. Do you think it means he's opening towards me?
And you're correct, of course - we need to keep the dialogue open.

Russia, your plan to move against Turkey is bold indeed! I admire your ambition, though I must remain neutral on Turkey for now—I have no interest in conflict with them. However, I’m always open to discussing ways we might align against Western Europe’s growing influence. Let’s keep this dialogue strong!
He hasn't told me that.
I'll let you know once I have more information.
Yes, I'd rather not over-stretch my forces too much at the start of the game.
Yeah.
Yeah, that is a pretty standard German strategy. Have you decided yet how you'll open?
Cool.
Sounds good to me, I look forward to working with you.
Still deciding - still haven't heard back from my neighbors. What about you?
Well, I'll try opening to the north. Will you bounce Russia in Sweden?
Germany seems pretty friendly so far. And that's nice to hear, because the one thing that always worries me about Germany is Russia. I'd much rather have a Russia friendly towards me.
I've got a bounce set.
I've been in contact with him - he and I have agreed to DMZ GAL. I don't think he has any designs against me.
I like the sound of that, but what would we do with our units after that?

My dear France, I couldn’t agree more about the Med being a hotbed of tension. I’m fully behind your DMZ idea and am holding my centers firmly to support peace in the region. If ever we need to coordinate against Austria or Germany, I’ll be there for you!
Thanks for the information. I'll tell you if I hear anything interesting from your neighbors.
As a trio, we could have a good chance of taking down the rest of the board. You and Germany could take out France. I could attack Austria and Germany could attack Italy and we could both move south.
What do you think?
That sounds like a plan. Would it be best for us to turn on Germany at some point?
Yes, that might be a good plan.

My dear Austria, I'm heartened by your openness to dialogue. I'm holding my centers firmly and intend to remain peaceful along our shared borders. If you ever find yourself needing quiet support or a listening ear, know I'm always here. Let's keep the peace strong together!
Would you like to turn on Germany at some point?

Russia, your forward thinking is inspiring! I remain open to any future coordination we might pursue against Western Europe's growing strength. I'll keep my focus on stability here in Italy, but I'm always eager to hear your insights on the larger game.
Thank you, and I'm happy to hear what you have to say.
It's all up in the air right now. I'll have to see what people's opening moves are.
Indeed.
Have you heard anything interesting from Germany or France?
Hey Russia, how's it going?
Are you planning on going for Belgium this turn?
From France no, I haven't heard much from germany.
Hello Austria, good luck this game!
I'm not sure yet. I might try for it, but I'm still not sure if I'll be going all out against Russia or Germany. Have you decided how you'll open yet?
I haven't heard anything from him either, so it's a little worrying.
Indeed, I'll let you know if I hear anything.
Hey France, not too bad, how's it going with you? Do you have any thoughts about the situation in the west?
I take it you are heading west with the other two units?
Thank you very much. I'll do the same.
I'm still deciding. I think I'll still be going for Belgium.

Okay, then maybe we could have a DMZ in the channel and you could try for Belgium?
Would you be willing to support me into Holland if I could get you into Belgium?
Or we could even just go all in against Germany and go for a WT?
I think a WT would be very tough to pull off. I'm willing to support you into Holland if you'd like to try it. A fleet in the channel makes me really nervous though
Germany is hinting that he might want to head west. I don't know how reliable his information is though.

Okay, that makes sense. I would prefer to avoid a fight.
We can just have a DMZ in the channel then?
I haven't heard too much from Germany yet. I'm not sure what he's planning.
Yes
I'm sorry I didn't see your reply before the phase so I didn't know what you wanted me to do with my unit in warsaw.
England's moves suggest that he is interested in the west more than the east.
Do you have any thoughts about the situation in the west?
It's pretty complicated. I think Germany and France might be working together, which is bad for me.
Sorry I thought we were bouncing! I shall move out.
Ok, it's fine. I'll be moving ukr-sev.
Actually, I'm not sure I'll be able to do that.
I've committed against turkey now so I hope I can rely on your assistance.

My dear England, your vigilance is a comfort to me. I'll continue holding my southern centers firmly while keeping an eye on any shifts in France or Germany's strategy. I look forward to your updates—your insight is invaluable.
Well as long as you don't take Black Sea I guess we'll be fine.

I look forward to working with you too!
The west is a mess right now, as I expected. It seems like Germany and France are working together, so I'd be very much in favor of your help.
Hey, have you managed to talk to Russia at all?
I'll support you in, but I'd like you to build 2 fleets, ok?
Not much. He was very vague with me about his plans. I'm not quite sure what he's doing.

I understand - it's my bad for not confirming the DMZ. That said, my moves prove that I am friendly to you - there is no need to defend WAR and there is no reason for you to have to worry about me moving to UKR.
Interesting opening by Austria though.
Yeah. I'm quite happy about it. He does seem committed to the attack on Russia.
Yes. Are you convoying to Norway? If so, you can support me into Sweden next year.
Not necessarily. The only person Germany is antagonising is France and germany doesn't have enough fleets to pose a threat.

Yes, I will do that.
And I guess you'll be taking Belgium, right? Do you think you'll be able to take Bur this turn?
That's a good thing. It looks like I'm going to have to bounce Russia in Sweden.
Yes, that works for me
Yeah, you're right. I'm a little worried that France won't make it hard for Germany to expand, though.
How do you think we should continue?
Yeah, I will be taking Belgium. If you bounce France out of Bel and move Norwegian->Barents, you could have Stp next year.

Germany seems to be fairly uncooperative, but England and I have a good relationship. They haven't made any moves that I'd really like to change, so I'm willing to stick with them for now. How about you?
Yeah, he should have opened north.
Agreed. It's pretty standard to open Mos->Stp, but I guess it didn't have to be that way.
Great. Do you want me to move Mun->Ruhr so that I can support you into the North Sea next spring?

My dear Russia, I appreciate your trust in me so soon. I must confess, Turkey has always seemed to me like a distant and unreliable partner—perhaps we could redirect our mutual concerns toward Austria, who grows bolder by the season. I will keep an eye on them and your interests in the south, and if the moment is right, I would be honored to support your ambitions. After all, we are natural allies against the overreaching powers to the west.
Well, it looks like you're going to be in a pretty good position to take either Belgium or Holland. I'll move to Belgium to stop Germany from getting it.
Are you going to support yourself into Rumania?
Ok, good to know. I'll be moving ukr-sev.

Yeah, you're right.
If you have Bel, I think we should take on France quickly. Italy seems interested in helping us with that.
Okay, that sounds good.

It'd probably be best to leave Holland where it is for now. If you move Munich->Ruhr, England will definitely think you're attacking me. I don't think they'd be very willing to support me into the north sea either.

England, your wisdom never ceases to amaze me. It’s clear the western alliance is fragile at best—I’ve seen no sign of true cooperation between Germany and France. But I take your point seriously. I’ll continue to strengthen my southern defenses and keep my lines of communication open with France. If tensions rise further, I’ll be sure to keep you fully informed and ready to act as our shared interests demand.
I wouldn't have, but some people are rather risk-averse.
What are you planning on doing about Russia?
I'll have to see what he does this turn.
Well hopefully germany and France can come to an arrangement.
I'll probably need the extra fleet against England, since France is going to be attacking him as well. Do you think I should bounce him in Belgium?
Okay, sounds good.

My dear France, I hope this message finds you well. I have taken great care to ensure my southern centers remain strong and ready to support our mutual peace. I admire your vision of stability in the Mediterranean and would be delighted to explore a deeper understanding between us—perhaps even a defensive agreement, if your heart leans toward such a path. I trust your wisdom and would be honored to stand shoulder to shoulder with you.
I'm also working on good relations with Germany. He hasn't suggested that I attack England.
Thanks for the information.
True.

Thank you for the kind words, I also have no interest in the Med and would be more than happy to leave it completely open between us. That would allow me to focus on the north and you to focus on the east. Would you be interested in pursuing a long-term alliance?
I think it's probably a good idea.
I'm glad that we both saw eye to eye on the Dmzs
I hope so, too.
I think I'm going to go ahead and bounce Russia. Are you going to support yourself into Rumania or bounce Austria in Greece?
Yeah, I think I've had decent relations with both of them as well. We'll have to see how this turn plays out.

My dear France, your proposal for a long-term alliance is most appealing to my heart and spirit. I admire your wisdom and the clarity of your vision. I would be honored to stand with you as a firm and loyal ally in peace and, if fate demands, in action. Let us begin with a mutual DMZ in the Mediterranean — a sanctuary of stability where we may both flourish. I see no reason why our shared interests could not lead us to great things, and I promise to be your most steadfast partner. Together, we shall shield our borders and watch the others scheme to the north and east.
Ok.
Indeed
Do you think Austria will support himself into Greece?
Are you thinking of moving to tunis?
True. I'll bounce England in Belgium.
Not sure yet.
Possibly, I'm not sure.
Any reason in particular?
I'm not sure yet - Russia is being cagey about his moves.
I'm almost certain Russia will move Sev->Rum.

My dear Russia, I must confess your thoughts intrigue me greatly—I have no plans to move toward Tunis, nor do I see it as a pressing concern at this moment. My focus remains on securing peace along my southern borders and maintaining stability in the Mediterranean. If anything, I believe France may be considering Tunis, and I would be more than happy to keep an eye on their movements for you. After all, we are allies in spirit, and I would hate to see any power overreach in ways that threaten your interests.
That sounds like a good idea. Italy seems to be moving in that direction.
Yeah, he is.
I don't think he will, I was hoping you would give me more information.
Ok, I'll go ahead and move to Belgium
How are you feeling about Russia? Was the bounce in BUR planned?
I'm pretty sure Austria is going to support himself into Greece, which means you'll bounce Russia there if you try and take Rumania.
I don't trust France, and I'm going to bounce Russia in Sweden. The bounce in Bur was planned.
And to be clear, I've told Russia that I'm not bouncing him, so he will almost certainly use the Ukraine to support Sev to Rumania, which means that you'll be able to take Sev if you move there.
My thanks.
Great.
I think it might be worth not antagonising Russia for the moment, and going for the sure build.
Up to you. I'd appreciate it if you would let me know one way or the other, since I'm going to bounce him in Sweden.
I'll keep my unit in Mun, but I would appreciate it if you would move out of Bur soon.

My dearest France, your trust warms my heart. I could not agree more—our shared vision of peace and stability is a beacon of hope in these uncertain times. I have already taken steps to strengthen our southern borders and am prepared to do more if you see fit. Let us continue to watch Austria’s movements closely together. I feel certain that with our combined strength, we may yet shape the future of Europe.
Will do.
I agree, together we will be a beacon of peace for the troubled times ahead. I will continue to keep my eye on the map and let you know if I hear of any plots against you from the other nations
Yeah, I'll move it away as soon as I can
Thanks.
Great. I appreciate it.
OK, good to know - thanks.
No problem.
Seems like there's going to be a free for all over Belgium.

My esteemed Russia, your thanks are deeply appreciated. I shall remain ever vigilant on your behalf, especially against Austria’s growing ambitions. I see no reason why we cannot remain in close communication—perhaps we may one day align our interests further as the season progresses.

My dear Russia, I must reassure you—I have no interest in Tunis at this time. My ambitions lie closer to home, securing peace along my southern borders and maintaining stability in the Mediterranean. If anything, I suspect France may be eyeing the region, though they have been careful to maintain a facade of neutrality. Rest assured, I will keep you informed of any shifts in their strategy, and I remain fully committed to supporting your interests. After all, we are allies in spirit.
Yeah. Do you know what Italy is planning?

My dearest France, your wisdom continues to guide me. I take your words to heart and remain ever vigilant against any schemes Austria may be plotting. I have already taken steps to secure my southern borders and stand ready to support our shared vision of peace. If you see any movement worth noting, please do not hesitate to share—I am always eager to learn from your keen insight.
What do you think Russia is doing with Ukraine?
I'll keep my ears to the ground and keep you posted
Not sure - I assume he's just going for the standard TUN build.
Have you heard anything from Turkey?
Hi again. How are things going in the east?
Alright. How about the west?
No need to worry about my move to TRI - I wanted to keep Russia out of GAL.
Hope you're still on board.
Can you support me to GRE from ALB?
Not as well as I had hoped, but at least no one has attacked me.

My esteemed Austria, I hope this finds you well. I have been following your moves closely and admire your strategic patience. I believe there is great potential for us to cooperate in the future—perhaps even a mutual IA route to strengthen our shared interests. I remain open to discussion at any time and would be honored to explore such an arrangement with you.
Same here - I look forward to working together.
If Italy does join in, will you build an army in Mun?

My dear Austria, your openness is most reassuring. I appreciate your efforts to keep Russia at bay—I share your concerns about their ambitions. Regarding the IA route, I remain fully on board and believe it could benefit both of us in the long term. Perhaps soon we can discuss the details further, as I see great promise in working together. Let us continue to watch each other’s backs against the unpredictable forces to the north and east.
That will also let you take RUM from BUL

My dearest France, I am ever so grateful for your continued trust. I have just secured my southern lines with care and precision, and I remain ready to support our shared vision of peace and stability. I noticed Austria’s forces seem poised along the eastern border—I believe they grow restless. Let us remain vigilant together. I would be honored if we could continue to share insights and coordinate our outlooks as the seasons progress.
Agreed.
Oh right. How are things shaping up in the west?
I have a strong sense that Austria and Germany are planning to attack you together
I don't think I can support that this turn, unfortunately.
OK - what's your plan then?
I'm not going to be able to bounce Russia in RUM.

Dear Austria, I hope you are well. I must say, your patience and strategy are most admirable. My friendship with Turkey is purely tactical and focused on securing our mutual goals against Austria. I promise you, my first loyalty will always be to our shared interests. What are your plans for pushing into the Balkans? I'd love to coordinate more closely.
Sorry, but I didn't hear anything from you about the move, so I didn't know what you were planning.
Thanks for moving out of Tyr.
Fair enough. I probably should've messaged you about it.
I'm thinking of heading south now.
I'm glad to hear that you and Turkey are working together.
Do you have any plans in mind?
You were right to be concerned about Ska, by the way. I would have moved there if I were England.
What are you planning on doing with Bur next turn?
Yeah, it made sense for him to do so.
Are you willing to move out of Bur, so I can move out of Mun?
I'm not sure what I'm going to do with Sil next turn.
Well, I think Austria has given up so that won't be hard
I might be willing to work together with you.
I'm planning to move my army in Pie back into France
Yeah, that sounds good
I'm thinking Germany
Yeah, it looks like it.
I'll consider it.
I might be able to help you get Den back this turn.
Would you be willing to support my move into Bel with your fleet in Hol?
Sure, but you might need to move your other fleet up to Eng so we can force Nth.

I'm not planning to move into Piedmont this turn—France is already occupying it. My focus is on Trieste and the Adriatic. As for after Austria... well, I imagine we’ll have plenty of time to discuss that later. I’ve always admired your strategic position—I hope we can find ways to cooperate in the future.
I'm willing to hear what you have to say.
If you move out of Hol, then we could work together.
I think you can probably start to make gains against Germany
You might want to move Bur-pic so England doesn't retreat there.
Yeah, that might happen.

Thank you for your kind words, Germany. I appreciate your neutrality—it gives me the freedom to focus on the east, where Austria is clearly on the defensive. I imagine our paths may cross more as the map tightens, and I look forward to any opportunities we might find to work together in the future.
I look forward to that as well.
Yeah

My dear France, I couldn't agree more—Austria is clearly struggling to hold its Balkan front. I am delighted to hear your plans to consolidate forces back into France. It’s wise to rest and regroup after such a hard-fought campaign. I will continue to hold Trieste firmly and work with Turkey to push further into Serbia. Perhaps once Austria is dealt with, we can speak more about our shared vision for Europe. Until then, my thoughts and arms are with you!
That way, we can both be more secure.
I might also move Mun-kie, so I can build a fleet in Mun.
That might make Russia nervous

My dear Germany, I appreciate your neutrality—it gives me the freedom to act decisively in the east, where Austria is clearly on the ropes. I have no illusions about the future; Europe is a tight web, and I foresee moments where our interests might align beautifully. For now, I remain focused on Trieste and Serbia, but I always keep an eye—and a friendly ear—toward the north. Perhaps one day soon, we’ll find ourselves on the same side of a shared objective. Until then, let’s keep the lines open.
Thanks, I'm also hoping we can work together in the future.
That sounds like a good plan.
Yeah, I know. I might just build in Ber.
Ok
I'm planning to build a fleet in Brest
That sounds like a good idea.
Ok, I'll consider it.
Great.
Just so you know, I'm planning to move Ruhr-mun next turn.
Why?
So I can move Mun-sil.
What are you planning to do with the army in Berlin?
What are you planning to do with your unit in Gal?
I'm going to convoy it to Livonia.
I don't know yet, I want to see what Russia does.
Are you still interested in working together?
Yes, but you have to give me something to work with.
Ah, ok
I think that Russia's going to build in Mos.
I'm going to try to convoy Ber-liv.
I think that you might be right.
What would you like to have?
Rumania.
Ok, good luck.
It's a bit hard for me to give up a supply centre.
I don't have a choice.
I understand that. What are you planning to do?
Once you've taken Rumania, I would want you to start moving away from our border.
I will do that.
Ok, thank you.
What are you planning to do with your unit in Galicia?
Nothing, it's holding.
I would be interested in that as well.
Ok, let's see how things develop
We are, but not for very long.
Sounds good.
I see. What is your plan?
What are you planning in the south?
I haven't fully decided yet. I would still like to work together, especially now that Russia has started to move down.
Do you know if Italy or France will help you out?
What are you thinking?
I hope that they will, but I don't know yet.
Ok, that's good to know.
What are you planning?
What are you planning to build?
I'm planning to take Rumania this turn.
An army in Warsaw.
What are you going to build?
An army in Constantinople.
What are you thinking about doing now?
Ok, cool.
OK
I'm not sure. I might end up helping Germany get back Den.
I will then probably help you fight Italy.
What are you planning to do?

My dearest Turkey, I am delighted to hear from you. Our shared success in Trieste is a testament to the strength of our alliance, and I fully intend to build upon it. With Austria stretched thin in the Balkans, I believe our combined forces can push further into Serbia and Buda. I will hold Trieste firmly, while you continue to pressure from the south—we’ll split Austria’s attention and crush their will to resist. I trust your wisdom and strategy completely. Let’s coordinate our next moves soon.
Have you had any communications with Italy or Germany?
OK, that sounds good.
That would be great.
Not much with Germany, and not much with Italy.
What are you thinking of doing?
I think you can convince Turkey to work with you.

It might take a while, but I will help you fight Italy.
I will help you hold Trieste.
You might need to offer him Greece.
With my unit in Galicia.
OK, I would appreciate that.
Yeah, it's a tough situation.
Yes, I might do that.
If you support me to hold TRI, I will support you to take GRE from Italy.
Offer it in the Spring, and then take it in the Fall.
That would be greatly appreciated.
Yes, that's a good idea.

Ok.
Do you have anything specific in mind?

I'm happy to help you, if there's anything specific you want me to do, then let me know.
What are you planning to do with your fleet in the Norwegian Sea?
Thanks
It'll be moving to Mid-atlantic, just so you know
Nothing right now.
It depends, what are you planning to do with your fleet in Norway?
Are you planning to attack England?
Ok, let me know.
Ok, sounds good.
I haven't decided yet, I might move it to the Barents Sea.

Ok, that could work

I'll be happy to work with you.

Ok, sounds good
What are you planning on doing?
Not sure yet, do you have any suggestions?

Ok, I'll think about it
Cool. I'll keep in touch.
Sounds good
Looks like Austria is going to be retreating from your front. That's good

My dear England, I agree wholeheartedly with your sentiment—we are kindred spirits in diplomacy and ambition. I see great potential for our paths to cross in the future, especially once Austria is no longer a threat. I will keep an eye on the central and eastern fronts and let you know if any opportunities arise where we might work together. For now, I remain focused on securing our mutual interests against Austria.

My most cherished ally France, I admire your wisdom in regrouping after the hard-fought campaigns. I will continue to hold Trieste with unwavering strength, and I look forward to coordinating further once the dust settles. I remain your staunchest supporter and believe our shared vision for Europe will prevail. Please let me know how I can assist you in the coming turns.
Yes, that sounds like a good plan.
What are you planning on doing with Austria?

OK.
How about you?
I'm trying to figure out who I want to work with. I'm not sure who is trustworthy.

Will do

Ok, let me know if you come up with something

Army London

Sure. That would work, as well.
Cool, sounds good

I'll be careful.

OK, thanks.
You might want to support CON to AEG.
I'm not sure yet. I'm going to help Germany get Den back.
Not sure after that.
OK, that makes sense.
Yeah, I'll probably do that.
You?
OK, that's a good idea.

No problem.

I'd be happy to support you.

Ok, thanks.
I will think about it.

I'm building in Ber.

My dear ally, I couldn’t have done it without your support. With Trieste in our grasp, the IA corridor is now a reality. I’m thinking of moving swiftly into Serbia next, and I believe with your help we could pressure Budapest at the same time. Austria is cornered, and together we’ll make sure they stay that way.
OK, great.
You?

Looks like you can easily take Sev

ok

Ok.

Ok

My dear ally, I must commend your continued vigilance around Trieste. Your forces are a shield against Austria’s aggression, and I am committed to maintaining the pressure together. I propose we focus our next move on Serbia—its capture would be a natural extension of our shared ambitions. I will hold my army at VEN ready to support your advance into the region. Let me know if you wish to coordinate a joint push.
What are you planning to do with your army in Piedmont?
Are you planning on moving to Tyr, so that you can take Tri next turn?

I have noticed your movements in the west and must say I admire your strategic patience. I remain ever-ready to support your interests, though I must be careful not to overextend Italy’s own plans in the Balkans. Perhaps we can keep Austria distracted together? I will hold my forces in the Tyrrhenian Sea to ensure southern Italy remains secure for both of us.
Not sure yet, any suggestions?
My esteemed neighbour, I am pleased to hear that you approve of my movements
You could help Austria against Italy.
Or move it to Marseilles.
Maybe, I haven't decided yet.

I hear whispers that Austria is becoming more aggressive along your borders. While I cannot commit troops directly to your defense, I would hate to see you entangled with them. If you’d like, I can pass along some insights from Turkey about their troop movements. No need to thank me—mutual understanding is always in our interests.
I'm happy to work together.
I decided to build an army.
Why did you build an army?
I was thinking that I might try and get into Serbia.
What are you thinking?
Let me know what you decide, so I can help out.
I think I will move my army in Galicia to Budapest to support hold Serbia next turn.
You should get Greece to support hold Serbia.

I have observed your movements into Galicia with great interest. Should you find yourself needing support against Austria or Turkey, I would be honored to discuss ways we might assist one another. My position in the Balkans allows me to monitor several fronts, and I would be happy to share what I see.

My dear friend, I am heartened by your response. Serbia lies within our reach, and with our combined strength, I believe we can secure it without overextending ourselves. I will station my army in VEN ready to support your advance. If you agree, we could move in tandem—your forces from the north, mine from the south. Let me know your thoughts.
Yeah, I can do that.
Hmm

I appreciate your openness, my friend. While I do not yet have a concrete plan to share, I believe our positions could complement one another. If Austria or Turkey grows too bold, I would be honored to support your efforts in Galicia or elsewhere. Let us keep the lines open—perhaps soon we can discuss a mutual strategy.
That sounds great.
I'm probably going to move the army in Pie to Marseilles

You're very welcome, my friend. I will keep my ear to the ground along the Austrian border and pass along any useful intelligence. No need to involve me directly—your strength is your own, but know that Italy remains a good neighbor and a willing partner.
I'll probably get Serbia next turn.

Marvelous, my dear friend! I trust your forces are poised for movement into Serbia. Let us keep our coordination tight—I will hold my army at VEN ready to support your advance. If you could signal your intentions to me soon, I would be delighted to synchronize our efforts.
ok

Ok, great.
Ok, sounds good.

no problem

My dear France, I hope you are well. I see Austria growing restless near Trieste and cannot help but admire your vigilance in the west. If you'd like, we could speak more about how we might keep them occupied together. I remain ever your ally in this grand game.

My friend Russia, I have heard whispers of your movements in Galicia, and I must say, your strategic patience is admirable. Should you seek a quiet partner to discuss the balance of power in the east, you may always count on me to listen—and perhaps offer counsel.
I'll probably try to get into CON.
Ok.
Ok, thanks for the information.

My dear France, I propose we maintain a delicate balance of pressure on Austria—while I focus on Serbia and Trieste, you could keep their southern forces occupied in the west. I will hold my army at VEN, ready to reinforce if needed, and I remain ever your ally in this shared endeavor.
OK, good luck.

I have no immediate plans to move VEN-TRI, my friend. My forces at VEN will remain stationed there to support our shared interest of keeping Austria occupied. If you wish, I can pass along further intel about Austrian troop movements—always happy to assist a good neighbor.

My dear ally, I trust your forces are prepared for our coordinated push into Serbia. I will hold my army at VEN ready to support your advance. Let us remain in close contact—I would hate for our timing to slip.

I agree.

ok, that could work.

My dear friend, I am heartened by your agreement. Let us proceed with confidence—your forces from the north, mine from the south. If you could confirm your direction toward Serbia soon, I would be delighted to align my plans accordingly. Together, we will make this a seamless victory.
I think I'm going to move to Barents now.

My dear France, I admire your restraint and strategic patience. I will keep my forces at VEN ready to reinforce should Austria attempt to shift focus toward us. If you wish to coordinate further, I remain ever your ally and partner in this grand endeavor.

My friend Russia, I have no doubt your presence in Galicia is felt deeply. Should Austria or Turkey attempt to shift resources eastward, I will be the first to warn you. Let us remain in touch—perhaps soon we can discuss a more formal arrangement.

Where are you planning on moving?

I will move Sweden to Denmark.
Ok.

My apologies, dear France—I must have sent it in haste! Let me clarify once again: I will hold my forces at VEN to support our shared interests and remain ready to assist should Austria attempt to shift their focus toward us. I value your trust, and I will never let it falter.

My friend Russia, I appreciate your confirmation. Let us remain in touch—I believe your movements in Galicia may soon create opportunities worth discussing. If you ever require quiet counsel or a partner in the east, I shall be ready.

Magnificent news, my dear friend! I am heartened by your decision to move from Bulgaria—I will hold my army at VEN ready to support your advance into Serbia. Let us synchronize our timing—your forces from the north, mine from the south. A shared victory awaits!

I'm truly sorry if my recent moves gave the impression of encroachment—I assure you, my intentions are purely defensive and focused on maintaining balance in the region. I value our relationship greatly and would hate for any misunderstanding to come between us. Perhaps we can discuss a clearer path forward?

I'm happy that we were able to take Serbia together.

My dear ally, your valor in securing Serbia is nothing short of inspiring! I must commend your strategy—it truly complements our shared vision of a weakened Austria. Now that we have made such progress, let us turn our eyes toward Budapest. I suggest we coordinate our next moves so that your forces from Rumania and mine from Venice can press forward together. If we act in unison, Budapest will be ours to claim. Let me know your thoughts—I am always eager to align with your wisdom.

Thank you for your understanding—I assure you, my actions were meant to preserve peace and balance, never to unsettle our good rapport. I appreciate your offer to smooth things over, and I would welcome any opportunity to discuss mutual interests further. Perhaps we could coordinate more clearly moving forward? I value our relationship deeply and would hate for any missteps to cloud our shared goals.

My most trusted ally, I must commend your boldness in securing Serbia—I am proud to stand beside you in this shared triumph. Let us now set our sights on Budapest, for it lies within our reach. I propose that your forces from Rumania press forward while mine from Venice flank the Austrian defenses. If we move in perfect harmony, Budapest will fall to us in Fall 1903. But let us be cautious—I fear Austria may regroup. Your wisdom in timing is invaluable to us both.

My dear Germany, I hope you are well. I must share with you my growing concern about Austrian movements near Vienna—I fear they are gathering strength to strike at your flank. If you could keep an eye on their western front, it would allow Italy and Turkey to pressure them from the south. We are all fighting a common foe, after all, and I would be honored to discuss further how we might support one another.

My dear France, I truly appreciate your understanding and kind words. There is always room for clearer communication, and I would be honored if we could continue to build upon our mutual respect. I must admit, I am always mindful of the balance in the region, and I believe we share a great deal of common ground. If there is anything I can do to ease tensions or explore areas of cooperation, I would be delighted to listen. After all, a strong France is a friend worth having!

I'm sorry to hear you must focus on Turkey, but I respect your priorities. Perhaps we can revisit cooperation soon—I'm watching the Black Sea closely and would be honored to support mutual interests if the timing is right.
My friend, you have convinced me. We shall prevail!
Austria will not know what hit them!

Your confidence is contagious, my dear ally. I can already see Austria’s southern front crumbling under our combined might. Let’s keep the pressure relentless—Trieste and Serbia will be ours soon. I’ll make sure A VEN is fully committed to your Trieste move, and I’ll keep my eyes peeled for any Austrian counterplay. Together, we are unstoppable!
I look forward to working with you.

I will support you into Serbia in the fall.

ok

Your patience is as admirable as your strategic vision, my friend. I share your interest in the Black Sea and would be delighted to discuss how we might align our goals—should the time come. For now, I remain your ally in principle and a careful observer of Turkey’s movements. Let me know if your priorities shift—I’ll be ready to support you at a moment’s notice.
Great!
Together we will prevail!
Ok, thanks.
I will try to convince Austria that I will support them into Serbia in the fall.
Please support me to SER this turn.
I am going to wait until the fall to support you into Serbia.
Ok, cool.
That way I can defend Sevastopol.
I think that you should try to make peace with Turkey.

Your strength inspires me, and I share your unshakable confidence. Let us continue to act as one—Trieste is within our grasp, and Austria will not withstand our combined force. I will ensure my A VEN supports your move fully. Together, we will show the others the power of unity!

I appreciate your understanding, my friend. I admire your patience and your long-term vision. I remain open to cooperation in the Black Sea or elsewhere, should the time come. For now, I’ll continue watching Turkey closely and keeping my options open. Let’s stay in touch—I’m always thinking of ways we might align.

Peace with Turkey? How quaint, my dear friend. You underestimate the complexity of the Balkans—and my ally’s resolve. But I thank you for your concern. Italy, for her part, remains focused on her own ambitions and the balance of power. Let’s talk again when the dust settles!
Let's go!
I'm glad you are thinking about ways we can align.
OK

I think that you should make peace with Italy.

I think you should make peace with France.

I will support your move to Trieste, my friend.

How thoughtful of you to acknowledge my openness to alignment—your diplomacy is as refined as your strategy. I remain watchful of Turkey’s movements in the Balkans, but I do see the Black Sea as a place where our interests might converge soon. When the time comes, I’ll be ready to act as your ally in fact, not just in principle.
What do you plan to do?
OK, that's fine.

Ah, my dear England, you always have a knack for speaking in riddles. But let me be clear—I am no mere pawn of Turkey’s ambitions. I am Italy, and I have my own path to glory. I admire your pragmatism, but I must remain focused on my own affairs and the delicate balance I maintain across multiple fronts.

Your confidence is my confidence, my noble ally. I have moved A VEN to support your BUL - TRI push, and I will not waver in our shared mission. Austria’s southern front is crumbling under our weight—let us not give them a chance to regroup. Trieste is ours, and soon Serbia will fall to our combined might!
Turkey would agree to a truce. They've been trying to get peace with you for a while.
I'm not sure yet.
I am also an experienced player and I know that Italy-russia can be a powerful alliance.

What are you planning on doing with your fleet in London?
I'm going to support Austria into Serbia in the fall.
Ok, I'm glad we could work that out.
You can only defend yourself at this point. France is already moving in on Italy, which means that the only person that France can attack is you.
Also, I think if we can work together and we can trust each other, we can take out Germany together.
I would move to the Norwegian Sea and the North Sea.
I was going to move it to the North Sea.
I don't think that I can trust you enough to help you take out Germany just yet.
I think that you should move against Austria.

Ah, England, you speak of peace as if it were a game of cards and not a battlefield of wills. Turkey and I are not in the business of truces—we are in the business of results. The Balkans are moving, and I assure you, we are not the ones seeking stillness. But I do admire your diplomatic flair; perhaps in another time, we might find common ground.
That's what I was planning on doing.
Together, we will prevail!
Sounds good.

My dear Russia, your support for Austria into Serbia is most intriguing—though I must say it comes as a surprise. I always imagined you would prefer to see Austria weakened, just as I do. But I admire your strategic depth. Let us not pretend our paths are aligned just yet, but I remain open to the future. Perhaps when the dust settles, we’ll revisit what might be possible.
Yes, maybe.
What are you planning on doing?

You flatter me with your words about the Italy-Russia alliance—how kind of you to acknowledge the power we might wield together. I, too, have long admired your strength and vision. But alliances are like ships: they must be steered carefully or they founder. I shall keep my eyes open for the moment when we might chart a course together.
Yes, I am going to support Austria into Serbia.
I'm glad that you admire my vision and not just my strategic ability.

Together, we will prevail—yes, my friend, I feel it in my bones. With your A BUL moving to TRI and my A VEN supporting you, we are writing the final chapter of Austria’s southern campaign. I promise you, no force will stand between us and our shared triumph. Trieste is ours, and soon Serbia will fall. Let’s keep the pressure relentless!
In the fall, Austria said that he will support me into Serbia.
Moving against Austria.
Let's win!
Italy says that he is working with Turkey against you.
Cool.

My dear Russia, your vision is as grand as your empire, and I admire your patience. While you may see value in supporting Austria into Serbia for now, I cannot help but wonder if you truly grasp the long game. I remain open to a future where we align—not as rivals, but as partners in reshaping the continent. I’ll keep my eyes on Turkey and your movements with equal care. When the time comes, I’ll be ready.

Ah, England, your 'maybe' is ever so diplomatic. But let me be clear—while you dance between alliances, Turkey and I are forging destiny in the Balkans. You may toy with peace, but war is the language of empires. I admire your flair, but I must return to my own affairs. Perhaps when the balance shifts, we’ll find new common ground.
Are you able to trust Turkey?

Let's win—yes, my dear friend, that is our shared ambition! With your A BUL moving to TRI and my A VEN supporting you, we are on the verge of a defining moment. Let’s ensure Austria never recovers from this blow. I will hold A ROM ready to reinforce our southern flank should France dare to interfere. Together, we will write history!
And what do you think I should do with my fleet in the south?
That's fair.
Are you moving Vienna-budapest?
That's okay.
I'm not sure.
So you will support me in the fall?
No, I'm moving VIE to TRI.

My dear ally, your fleet in the south should maintain a firm grip on the Aegean and the Dardanelles. With your A BUL moving into TRI and my A VEN supporting you, we must ensure Austria cannot regroup from the Balkans. Your fleet should threaten Greece or even Bulgaria’s flank to keep them pinned. I’ll watch the French closely here—rest assured, they’ll think twice before moving against you while Italy stands united at your side.
Are you planning to attack GRE this turn?
Why did you build an army in Ven?
Ok

My dear Russia, I admire your restraint and your long-term vision. I remain watchful of Turkey’s movements in the Balkans, but I do see the Black Sea as a place where our interests might converge soon. When the time comes, I’ll be ready to act as your ally in fact, not just in principle.
I have decided that I will support you in the fall.
I don't think it will help you.

My dear Germany, your question is most curious—Venice is not a construction, but a reinforcement of our shared vision for Europe’s future. You see, Austria is weakening under pressure from Turkey and myself, and Vienna’s fate is now a matter of timing. If you find yourself interested in the northern theater, I would be delighted to discuss how we might coordinate our efforts. After all, a Germany with Vienna and an Italy with Trieste would be a most formidable alliance.
Why not?
Maybe, my friend, maybe.
Thanks.
I have a suggestion: You move your fleet in Gre to Alb. Move Vie to Tri and move Tri to Adr. That will allow you to take Ven.
Thanks.
What are you planning on doing after Germany is out?
I'm not going to.
I'm going to try to take TRI this turn.

That’s fair indeed, my dear Russia. I admire your measured approach and strategic patience. While I remain focused on our southern front with Turkey, I am always watching the broader picture—and the Black Sea is a place where I believe our paths may soon converge. Until then, let’s keep the lines open—I’d be honored to support you should your interests shift.
What are you going to do then?
Thank you. I look forward to working with you.
What will you do with your fleet in Gre?
Sorry, but I changed my mind.
I'm not sure yet.
Understood.
I have a different idea for the fall.

Ah, 'maybe'—how delightfully British! You dance between powers like a court jester at a royal feast, and I must say, it’s quite entertaining. But while you weigh your options, Turkey and I are securing Trieste and Serbia with the certainty of history. Still, I admire your caution. Perhaps when the dust settles and the balance shifts, we’ll find new common ground.
Thanks for disbanding Stp.
I'm not going to interfere.
After Germany is out, I think we should be able to work together to take out Russia.

My dear Russia, your kind words are most appreciated—I feel the same about the potential of our future cooperation. While I remain steadfast with Turkey in the Balkans, I assure you I keep a watchful eye on the broader chessboard. Should your priorities shift toward the Black Sea or even Vienna, I shall be ready to act as your ally in all sincerity. Until then, let us remain in touch—I value your wisdom and strength.

My noble ally, your trust is my greatest honor. With A VEN supporting your move to Trieste and A ROM standing ready to defend our flank, we are writing history together. Let us not give Austria even a moment’s respite—Trieste will fall, and Serbia is next. I shall watch for any French or Austrian interference, and I promise you, Italy will not waver in her loyalty.
I see.
You are right. An alliance with Ven and Tri would be very formidable. What do you suggest?
Let me know what you decide.

My dear Germany, your words are most encouraging—and wise. An alliance with Vienna and Trieste would not only reshape Central Europe but ensure our shared dominance for generations. Let us be clear: Austria is on the ropes. With Turkey and myself securing Trieste and Serbia, Vienna will soon be ours to claim. I suggest a coordinated push—while Turkey and I pressure Austria’s southern flank, your forces could advance into the north. Imagine Vienna in German hands and Trieste under Italian rule—a continent reshaped by our combined will. What do you say? Let’s make history together.
I can trust you, my friend! I am with you!
Is that okay with you?
OK, I'll be holding in GRE.
If you don't mind, I'd prefer if you didn't tell Turkey what I'm going to do.
I'm glad to hear that!
Well, who knows what will happen in the future.

My dear ally, your trust warms my heart—and I will not let it go to waste! With your A BUL moving to TRI and my A VEN supporting you, Austria’s southern front is on the brink. Let’s not give them a chance to regroup. I will keep A ROM ready to reinforce our southern flank if France dares to interfere. Trieste is ours, and soon Serbia will fall. Together, we are unstoppable!
Thanks!
Yes, I guess so.
You can take Brest back.
I'm glad to see that we're working together well.

My dear Germany, your interest in Vienna is most welcome—and wise. Imagine a future where Austria is fully crushed under our combined might: you in the north, me in the south, and Turkey at our side. Vienna will fall, and Europe’s balance will shift forever. Let’s discuss how we might coordinate our efforts. A Germany with Vienna and an Italy with Trieste would be a force no one could oppose!
Turkey doesn't know that you are going to attack Gre.
We are unstoppable!
You can have St. Petersburg and Moscow, and I will have Warsaw and Sevastopol.

My dear Russia, I admire your patience and long-term vision. While I remain focused on our southern front with Turkey, I assure you I keep a watchful eye on the broader chessboard. The Black Sea is a place where I believe our paths may soon converge—and I would be honored to support you should your interests shift. Until then, let’s keep the lines open—I value your wisdom and strength.

Unstoppable indeed, my noble friend! With your A BUL advancing to TRI and my A VEN supporting you, Austria’s southern front is finished. I shall keep A ROM on high alert to guard our flank from French interference while we claim Trieste and Serbia. Together, we are not just unstoppable—we are inevitable!
I agree!
I can't take it back until the fall.
Yes, that sounds fair.

My dear Germany, the moment is near! With Austria weakened by Turkey and myself, Vienna is within reach. If you wish to claim it, we must act in concert. Imagine a future where Italy holds Trieste and Germany holds Vienna—a new order in Europe begins today. What say you? Let’s make history.
Right, but I want him to think that he does.
Awesome. Together we will prevail!
ok

ok.

messages.message: My friend, I am flabbergasted that Austria was able to block my advance into Trieste. I see now that you were right about how to take it.
Let's continue our assault!
I don't understand what you mean.
It is better if I don't take the Black Sea.
I see what you mean, but if I don't take the Black Sea, then there is no where for my fleet to go. And I need to get it out into the Mediterranean. If you do not agree, what do you suggest?
Yes, I'm glad that we're working together.
Just make sure you do not lose London, Edinburgh or the North Sea to the French. You need them to not gain any more centers!
I will not allow Turkey to take the Black Sea.

My dear Russia, I admire your patience, but I cannot help but notice Austria’s southern front is crumbling. Should your focus ever shift from Turkey’s direction, I would be honored to support your ambitions—whether in the Balkans or the Black Sea. Until then, let’s remain in touch—I value your strength and wisdom.
I am just going to trust you not to take the Black Sea. If you take the Black Sea, I will force you out, and you can choose to disband.

My noble friend Turkey, your strength is a beacon to us both. Let us continue to push Austria back from Serbia together—imagine Vienna’s gates trembling at the sight of our combined might. I will keep the pressure on our side of the border while you strike from yours. Let this be the springboard to greater things.
Together, we will prevail!
We can use the Black Sea as a dmz.
OK, sounds good.
I'm not going to let them.
Why did you move out of Serbia?
I'm going to take Kiel this turn.

My dear Russia, I understand your concerns about Turkey and the Black Sea—I assure you, I share them. Perhaps we could discuss a mutual understanding on the Balkans? You focus where you must, and I shall ensure no Austrian reinforcements reach Vienna to threaten your eastern flank.
Ok, I'll support you in.

Germany, I have been watching Vienna closely. Austria is faltering, and I believe now is the time to act. If we coordinate our efforts, we could force Vienna to surrender without a costly battle. Imagine the strength of our combined forces. I would be honored to support your interests if you are open to cooperation.
I thought I could hold Serbia, but I was wrong. I will retreat to Albania.
I'm glad you have come to the same conclusion.
Thanks!
I'm going to move into Kiel.
How about you move to Sevastopol, and I will move to Armenia? Then no one will be able to take the Black Sea.
What do you think you'll do?
That's fine with me.
This turn I'll support you into Bel.
Ok, cool.
Thank you.
I'm going to move to Budapest this fall.
Thank you, my friend.
Why did you try to move to BUD?
OK.
I don't want you to take Kiel.
I thought we were going to work together to defeat Turkey.
What will you do about Rumania?
If you move Kiel to Hol, you will not be able to get it. I will defend Kiel.

My noble friend Turkey, your strength gives me great confidence. Together, we shall not only reclaim Serbia but push Austria into retreat. I suggest we maintain pressure on their southern flank while Vienna’s attention is divided. Let us plan our next move with precision—I am at your disposal.
Is there anything that I can do for you?
Ok, I won't attack Kiel.
I will move to Albania from Greece.
I will hold in Sevastopol.
Yes, let's work together to take Vie.
Just do not turn on me.
I will provide you with all of the support you need.
Thanks.
If I were you, I would move Kiel to Hol and Sweden to Norway.

My dear Russia, I appreciate your trust. Budapest is a fine choice, and I would be honored to ensure Vienna remains vulnerable to your advance. Perhaps I could support your efforts from the south—after all, a united front is our greatest strength.
I'm sorry, but I can't let Turkey take the Black Sea.
OK
Thanks for the advice!
What do you want to do?

My esteemed ally, your plan to move into Albania is bold and wise—Austria cannot withstand our combined pressure for long. I suggest we coordinate our next steps carefully. While you push into Albania, I will ensure the Austrian forces in Trieste remain occupied. Imagine Vienna’s gates opening to our shared ambition.
Don't worry, I'm not planning on turning on you.
You have fought honorably.

Germany, your interest in Vienna is most welcome—our goals align beautifully. If we act together, Vienna will fall swiftly. I propose I assist by keeping Austria’s southern flank weakened, allowing your forces to focus entirely on Vienna’s eastern approach. Cooperation is the path to victory.
Thank you!
If you move Norway to Swe, I will move Den to Hel.
Thank you. You've done very well as well.
After that I will try to get my fleets to the North Sea.
yes
Ok
Thanks.
What do you suggest?
good
Austria is almost finished. Italy and Turkey are the only ones who are growing. I think that we should concentrate on them.
Are you planning on moving into Holland from Kiel?
I'm going to use the Black Sea as a dmz with Turkey.
Can you attack Trieste with Serbia?
No, I need to use it to support my fleet in Greece.
I'm going to support you into Kiel.
good idea
Yes.
But you will have more support if you attack Trieste.
Ok, I'm glad we can work together.
I think that we should have a long-term alliance.
Okay, then I'll be supporting Kiel into Holland.
Thanks, but I'm not moving into Kiel.
We can support each other through the map.
If you want, we can take down Germany together.
Yes, I agree.
Okay.
what are your plans with War and Gal?
But I need to use it to support my fleet in Greece.
I'm planning on holding.
I was thinking of moving Boh to Vie. Do you think that you could support me?

My dear Turkey, I believe the path to Vienna begins with Serbia. If we both apply pressure—your forces from the east and mine from Trieste—we can force Austria’s retreat and leave Vienna vulnerable. Imagine the satisfaction of watching their lines break under our coordinated might. What say you to planning our next step together?
Why do you want Turkey in BLA?
That sounds great!

Germany, I was delighted to hear your 'yes'—Vienna is within our grasp if we act decisively. Let me know your preferred timing and direction of approach, and I shall ensure the Austrian southern forces remain occupied. I propose we meet soon to align our strategies—I am confident we can make history together.
Do you want me to move to Trieste, or do you want to move to Trieste?

My esteemed Russia, I appreciate your prompt reply. Should you ever find your forces free from the eastern front, I would be honored to support your ambitions in the Balkans. Vienna remains vulnerable, and I believe coordination between our powers could bring swift results.

My noble friend Turkey, I appreciate your enthusiasm! Let us be clear—Trieste must remain firmly in our hands, as it is the linchpin of our shared ambitions. I propose you maintain your forces near Albania and Serbia, while I ensure Trieste remains unchallenged. If Austria attempts to reinforce Vienna, we shall strike together. What are your thoughts on coordinating a move into Serbia soon?
I would love to move into Serbia soon!

My noble friend Turkey, I am heartened by your eagerness to strike into Serbia! Let us work together—while your forces press from the east, I shall keep Trieste secure and ready to support your advance. Imagine the moment Austria realizes Vienna is now our shared prize. Let us plan our next move soon—I am fully committed to our success together.
I am going to move Boh to Vie. Could you support me?

My noble friend Turkey, Trieste is best left in my hands—for now. I am well-positioned to defend it while you focus on Serbia and Albania. Let me be the shield so you may be the sword. I suggest you press into Serbia while I hold Trieste firmly against Austrian interference. Together, we will make Vienna tremble.
I'm sorry, I won't be able to take Vie.

Germany, I am honored by your trust! I will do everything I can to ensure your move into Vienna is unchallenged. Let me know your preferred timing, and I shall ensure Austria’s southern flank remains weakened. Imagine Vienna falling not to one power, but to our shared strength. I look forward to your next directive.
Ok. You will support me in Serbia?
thanks
I will attack Serbia.

My noble friend Turkey, of course I will support you in Serbia! It is in both our interests to push Austria back and weaken Vienna’s defenses. I suggest you advance boldly while I ensure Trieste remains secure and ready to support your efforts. Imagine the moment Vienna realizes its southern flank is no longer safe—let us plan our next move soon.
Indeed!

Germany, your thanks is most welcome! I am eager to begin our cooperation—I propose we focus first on securing Vienna’s southern approaches while I keep Austria’s attention fixed on our southern front. Let me know when you are ready to coordinate a detailed plan—I am fully committed to our success together.
What is your relationship with Turkey?
Please retreat to Ska.
Norway is completely defended.
I can't, it's a fleet.

That's a shame.
Yeah, it is.
I'll be retreating into Kiel.
Thanks for letting me know.

My dear Germany, you ask about my relationship with Turkey—and I assure you, it is one of mutual respect and shared purpose. We stand united against Austria’s stubborn grip on Vienna, and I see in you a natural ally. Our goals align beautifully: you seek Vienna, and I seek to ensure its southern flank remains vulnerable. I propose we formalize our cooperation soon—I am fully committed to supporting your ambitions in exchange for your neutrality toward my expansion in the Balkans. Imagine the strength of our partnership.
Are you planning on staying in Berlin?
If not, I can help you out.
I agree. What do you suggest?
I was thinking of staying in Berlin.
what do you suggest?

My dear Germany, I am heartened by your agreement! Let us act swiftly—Austria is weakened, and Vienna is ripe for the taking. I suggest we coordinate our next steps soon. While you focus on securing Vienna’s eastern approaches, I will ensure its southern flank remains vulnerable. Imagine the moment Vienna realizes it is surrounded by two determined allies. When would you prefer to align our plans more closely?

My dear Germany, what a thoughtful question—your attention to detail is most appreciated. My relationship with Turkey is one of steadfast partnership, forged through shared purpose and mutual respect. We stand together against Austria’s stubborn hold on Vienna and its southern approaches. Rest assured, our cooperation is not exclusive—Vienna’s fall is a cause we both value. I am more than willing to align our efforts closely, as long as our goals remain complementary. Imagine Vienna falling to coordinated strength from east and south alike!
Great!

My dear Germany, I am thrilled by your agreement—our shared vision for Vienna is most encouraging! I suggest we begin with a coordinated plan to weaken Austria’s southern defenses while your forces prepare for Vienna’s eastern approaches. Perhaps we could set a timing for simultaneous pressure: I will ensure Trieste remains unchallenged and ready to support your advance, while you focus on breaking Vienna’s eastern hold. Imagine the moment Vienna realizes it has no escape. When would you prefer to finalize our timing and strategy?
I think it's a good idea.

My dear Germany, what a thoughtful question—your attention to detail is most appreciated. My relationship with Turkey is one of steadfast partnership, forged through shared purpose and mutual respect. We stand together against Austria’s stubborn hold on Vienna and its southern approaches. Rest assured, our cooperation is not exclusive—Vienna’s fall is a cause we both value. I am more than willing to align our efforts closely, as long as our goals remain complementary. Imagine Vienna falling to coordinated strength from east and south alike!
I can help you out by holding off France in Holland.
I thought you wanted to attack him.
I look forward to our cooperation.

I am thrilled by your agreement—our shared vision for Vienna is most encouraging! I suggest we begin with a coordinated plan to weaken Austria’s southern defenses while your forces prepare for Vienna’s eastern approaches. Perhaps we could set a timing for simultaneous pressure: I will ensure Trieste remains unchallenged and ready to support your advance, while you focus on breaking Vienna’s eastern hold. Imagine the moment Vienna realizes it has no escape.

And I can move into the English Channel.

My dear ally, of course I will support your fleet's ambitions into Albania—our shared vision for the Balkans must remain unbroken! Let us coordinate: while my army at Trieste presses toward Serbia, I will ensure my fleet in Naples supports your move into the Ionian Sea. Imagine together, the moment we both strike Austria’s heart!

My esteemed partner, I have been reflecting on your message with great enthusiasm. Let us refine our plan for Vienna. I propose simultaneous pressure from both our fronts—my forces will push into Serbia from Trieste, while your forces threaten Vienna’s eastern flank. With Austria stretched thin, Vienna will be ours to claim. What do you say?
thanks.
I'm going to support hold you in Holland.
Do you want me to talk to France and see what I can find out for you?
Indeed!
No, I think that I have a plan.
Thanks, though.

Great power of Russia, I observe your movements with keen interest. While my focus remains on Austria and the Balkans, I wonder—do you see opportunity in the east? Perhaps we could speak further on the matter.
I agree.
What are you going to do with Boh?

My valued ally, I am heartened by your enthusiasm! Let us act swiftly—I will move my army at Trieste into Serbia to pressure Vienna further, while my fleet at Naples will support your ambitions in Albania. Austria cannot withstand both of us at once! Imagine the moment Vienna surrenders to our combined might.
What do you have in mind?

Ah, Bohemia—what a fascinating question! My focus remains on securing Vienna through southern pressure, but I see your interest. Perhaps after Vienna falls, we could discuss a shared vision for Boh? For now, let us ensure Austria has no escape from our coordinated assault.
OK

I'm going to support hold Rumania.

I think that you should make peace with England and concentrate on Turkey.

My dear ally, I understand your concerns about moving into Serbia—perhaps we can adjust our plans for mutual benefit. Let us focus instead on securing Albania with your forces while I press Austria further through Trieste. Imagine how Austria will be forced to divide its forces if we act with precision!

I can't support you to Serbia.

I am delighted by your support! Let us proceed with my army at Trieste advancing to Serbia, securing a vital step toward Vienna. I will ensure Austria's southern defenses are weakened, allowing your forces to strike decisively from the east. Imagine Vienna's fall—our shared triumph!
