Are you working with England?
I have an agreement with him that we won't attack each other for now.
We have to stop him before he becomes too powerful.
Maybe I can talk to Turkey.
OK, I see.
I can help you with Italy.

You can try to take Naples and Greece.
OK, I'm going to try to take Greece and Naples this turn.
I will try to take Warsaw, and France might take Tunis.
Sounds good!
That's what I'm doing.
I have talked to Turkey, and he said that he is going to try to take Nap and Gre.
If you attack Germany, Russia will probably be able to take BER.
Why did you build in London instead of Edinburgh?
I'm not going to attack Germany yet.
We should still wait though.
We have to be careful though.
We need to watch out for Russia.
OK
We need to be careful though, because Russia might move to Livonia.
I'll watch out for that.
He is the weakest.
Thanks.
He might try to take Berlin.
He might also move his army from Warsaw to Livonia.
And I have more units over him.
Yes, but he's still dangerous.
I guess I just didn't think about it.
I'm moving to the Norwegian Sea and the North Sea.
He can't support BER from PRU.
He might move MOS to WAR, though.
Are you going to move from the North Sea to Norway?
He could move from Prussia to Warsaw, though.
Germany told me that Turkey is going to take Greece next turn.
I can support you if you do.
Do you need help with RUM?
Turkey should be able to take GRE next turn.
Yes, that would be great.
Yes, I know.
Which army do you plan on using to take Warsaw?
You were right. My heart was not in this game.
What did Germany say?
I will move to Livonia and Sil.
Do you want to support BUD into RUM?
Yes, but I think that I can handle him.

My dear ally, I’ve noticed your forces shifting north. Let’s be clear—our shared goal remains the Balkans. I’m keeping Rome and Naples secure against France’s every move, just as promised. If Austria tries anything, we’ll be ready. I trust you’ll support me in Serbia soon.
I need more supply centers before I help you in Serbia.
But I need more supply centers before I can help you in Serbia.

I’ve no interest in conflict with you, my friend. My focus is firmly on Austria and securing Rome. If you move toward NAP, I’ll have to act in defense—but I’d rather see us avoid a clash. Let’s talk soon.
OK, sounds good.
Yes, that's a good idea.
I won't go for Naples.

I understand your position, my friend—I respect your need for strength. Let’s not let this delay our shared vision. If you need time, I’ll hold the line against Austria and France in the south. I propose we focus on stabilizing Serbia together once you’re ready. I’ll keep the Adriatic clear for you—just let me know when you’re in a stronger position.
He says that Turkey will probably move to Greece.
You're right.
You could move to Albania.
We have to stay allied to each other though, because if we fight, it will become Turkey vs. us.

I’m glad to hear you won’t press toward Naples—at least for now. I’m more than willing to discuss a mutual understanding on southern Italy. Perhaps we could find common ground against Austria? I’ll keep my forces focused on the east and Adriatic, where they belong.
OK, sounds good!
Yes, I am going to move to Albania.
Or you could try to take Rumania.
Will do.
That doesn't really surprise me.
Yes, we can't let them beat us.
Me too.
He also said he might try to move to Albania.
OK, thanks!
We could try to take down Turkey together.
You're welcome to go after Italy all you want.
I am going to do both.
I'm trying to convince France to attack Italy.
OK, good luck.
Thanks!
Thanks.
I'm trying to do that, too.
I think he will.

My dear ally, I truly appreciate your confidence in me. I’ll keep the Adriatic clear for you and ensure that Austria and France remain preoccupied. If you're ready to act on Serbia soon, just let me know—I’m more than prepared to support you. No need to rush; I’ll hold the line until the moment is right.
OK, thank you very much.
I probably won't take anything though.
I think so, too.
We just have to be careful that Germany and France don't become too strong.
I'm sure you'll get MAR.
I will try to convince Turkey to help us.
I don't know if Germany will let you keep BEL.
You should help us with Russia.
Yes, we need to be careful.
OK, I will do my best.
Yes, I'll get Marseilles.

Ah, a joint effort against Turkey? A bold idea, my friend. But I must be honest—I see more opportunity in uniting against Austria’s weakened state. Imagine what we could accomplish together in the Balkans. Of course, I understand your interests, and I’m open to discussion—let’s talk soon.
Do you think we should include France in our alliance?
I'll have another unit there.
You can have as many units as you want in Italy.

Of course, my dear ally—thank you for your trust. I understand your need for patience, and I will continue to keep Austria and France occupied while you consolidate your strength. I’ve no doubt we’ll soon be ready to act together on Serbia. Until then, I remain your firm partner in the south.
You can take Rumania and Sevastopol.
That's what I'm going to do.
Thank you very much!
France and I have been helping each other against Italy.
Great!
You might have to convince him to move against Germany.
Thank you!
You're right, that's probably easier.
I don't think so.
We will have to decide how to divide Italy's supply centers after we defeat him.
What will you do this turn?
Do you think you'll be able to hold on against Germany?
That might be difficult.
Yes.
I think that I'll be able to.
It might be a good idea for us to try to work together again.
I'm moving from the North Sea to Edinburgh.

Of course, my dear ally—I’m always here for you. This turn, I intend to hold TRI firmly and keep the Adriatic safe for your ambitions. Austria is weak, and Germany is moving boldly into Bohemia and Vienna. I suggest we focus our combined strength on Serbia soon, when the moment is right. Until then, I’ll ensure nothing disturbs our shared interests.
Do you know if France is willing to help you?
Can you let me have Warsaw?
I can't help you take it.
I think so.
OK, good luck!
What are you asking?
I mean support hold War.
OK.
I will take SEV this turn.

Thank you for your confidence, my dear ally. I will hold TRI and VIE firmly, ensuring no interference from Austria or France. I look forward to our coordinated move on Serbia soon. For now, I will support your push to SEV—let’s keep the pressure on Turkey’s eastern flank.
Sounds good!
I'm worried that France might be working with Germany.
I don't think that they are, but I'm not sure.
Thanks!

My dear ally, I truly appreciate your confidence—I always have your back in the Adriatic. I will hold TRI and VIE firmly, and if Austria dares to push south, I’ll meet them with steel. I’ve also seen Germany’s moves into Bohemia; if they take Vienna, I’ll be in a strong position to support your ambitions in the Balkans. Let’s talk soon about coordinating pressure on Serbia once the dust settles.
Do you think we should include France?
I've talked with him a little.
With Germany?
He hasn't mentioned Germany.

Thank you, my dear ally—I appreciate your confidence. I’m holding TRI and VIE firmly, and if Austria dares to push south, they’ll find me ready. I’ve also seen your move toward SEV—clever and bold. Let’s keep the pressure on Turkey’s eastern flank while I ensure the Adriatic remains clear for you. I look forward to our next coordinated move soon.

Sounds good! I appreciate your trust, my dear ally. I will hold TRI and VIE firmly, ensuring Austria remains distracted while you consolidate strength in the east. I look forward to our coordinated move soon. Until then, I’ll keep the Adriatic clear for you.

Thank you, my dear ally—I truly appreciate your confidence. I’m holding TRI and VIE firmly, and if Austria dares to test me, they’ll find me more than ready. Your move on SEV is bold and welcome—I’ll keep the Adriatic clear so you may focus on the east. Let’s talk soon about coordinating pressure on Serbia once the dust settles.

My dear ally, I'm heartened by your 'sounds good' — I take it as a sign we're aligned. I'll be holding TRI and VIE with an iron grip, and I've no intention of letting Austria or France disrupt our plans. I've also noticed your bold moves toward SEV — clever positioning indeed. Let me know when you're ready to coordinate a push into Serbia — I'm fully committed to supporting your vision. Until then, I'll keep the Adriatic safe for you.

My dear ally, I truly appreciate your kind words — 'good luck' is a promise I take seriously. I will hold TRI and VIE with vigilance, ensuring nothing disrupts your bold move on SEV. I look forward to hearing of your success and preparing for our next coordinated step. Until then, I remain fully committed to your cause and our shared ambitions.

Your bold push toward SEV is a masterstroke—I’ll keep the Adriatic clear so you can focus entirely on your eastern ambitions. Let’s talk soon about coordinating a move into Serbia once the moment is right. Until then, I remain fully committed to your cause and our shared vision.

I will support you to Vie.

I appreciate your restraint near NAP—for now, my friend. I’ll continue to focus on Austria and Turkey’s flank, keeping southern Italy secure. If you ever wish to discuss cooperation, I’m always open to a conversation. Until then, let’s avoid unnecessary conflict.
Italy is on the verge of collapse!

Thank you, my dear ally—I take your 'sounds good!' as both encouragement and a promise of future coordination. I will hold VIE firmly and push into Bohemia or Tyrolia as needed to support your advance. If Austria tries to resist, we’ll crush them together. Let’s solidify our plans soon—I’m ready to act.
So do you want me to support you to Vie?

My dear Germany, yes—please support me to VIE. That’s exactly the coordination we discussed. With your support, we’ll crush Austria’s last resistance and claim Vienna together. I’ll hold VIE firmly and push into Bohemia or Tyrolia as needed. Let’s make sure Austria knows they cannot stand against us.
Yes, so I am supporting you now?

My dear Germany, I appreciate your support—yes, you are supporting me now! That is precisely the coordination we need to strike Austria at Vienna. I will hold VIE firmly and push into Bohemia or Tyrolia as needed. With your strength in KIE and my hold on Vienna, we’ll crush their last resistance. messages.message: I think that he might be, but I'm not sure.
Germany just stabbed him.
Yeah, I know, that's why I'm not so sure.
I thought you were going to move into RUM.
I can move out and let you have it next turn.
Yes, I'm going to take Naples next turn.
Turkey can probably take either Naples or Tunis.
Did you know he was going to move to LON?
Italy is pretty much done.
No, I don't think we should include France.
Sorry I didn't tell you I was going to move to the English Channel.
Turkey is in a much better position than Italy.
Ok, I'm moving to London this turn.
From the North Sea.
I'll support you into ION this turn.
He's also in a good position to take Greece and Serbia.
No, I didn't know, but I don't think that it's too big of a deal.
I'm not going to attack him though.
I think that I can handle it.
I think we should try to work together against Turkey.
I don't know if he's working with Germany, though.
You should move to London this turn.
OK, thanks.
So I can take Norway.
Yeah, I'm not sure either.
I think France is going to attack you soon.
Okay, I'll support your move from the North Sea.
You'll still need to use your fleet in Edinburgh to move to London though.
You should ask Russia for help.
Yeah, I know.
You should try to convince Italy to help you defend against Turkey.
It would be much too difficult.
Why?
Of course not.
Okay.
Thanks for the support.

My dear France, I appreciate your offer to work together—truly, I admire your strength and vision. But let us be honest, our interests in the western Mediterranean remain at odds. I would gladly avoid conflict if you would allow me Naples in exchange for a truce. However, I must warn you—Germany grows restless. I’ve seen their fleets moving north, and I fear they may soon turn their attention to your eastern flank. Perhaps we could speak more directly about how to avoid provoking their ire.
Why did you move your fleet from the North Sea to Holland?
I know he's been working with you, but he could easily take BEL and HOL.
Italy doesn't respond.
He finally responded to me.
What did he say?
I'm not sure whether I'll do that this turn. I don't want to take any unnecessary risks.
I'll see what I can do.
He wants revenge on Germany.
He wants a truce.
I will talk to him.
Maybe you two could work together against France.
That's fine.
I will agree to give you Naples in exchange for a truce.
Watch out for France. He can take Tunis and Nap at the moment.
You should also talk to England. Maybe you two could work together against France.
Ok, I might do that.
Just for protection. I'm a little jumpy right now. I will move it back to Hel.
Do you think he'll try to take MAR?

My dear France, how kind of you to consider such an arrangement. Naples is indeed a prize of great strategic value, and I admire your pragmatism. However, I must tread carefully—I cannot betray Germany’s trust so soon after our coordination on Vienna. Perhaps if we delay the truce until Spring 1907, I could make good on my promise to Naples and still maintain my delicate balance with Berlin. I ask only for time, and in return, I will warn you of German movements in the Rhine should they ever turn their gaze toward your heartland.
I was thinking the same thing.
Let me know what you decide.
Okay, I understand.

Germany, my valued ally, I must share troubling news from Austria’s general staff. They are preparing a two-pronged assault—one aimed at your northern lines near Berlin, the other toward Vienna. I suggest we strengthen our coordination in Vienna’s defense while we prepare to strike back from the south. Imagine the delight of Austria’s generals when their own forces turn on one another. I believe we could even lure France into a distraction in the west if we play our cards right.
Germany told me that he's going to move his fleet from Holland to the North Sea.
Don't worry, I will move it back to Hel.
I don't know how you'll be able to survive both of them attacking you.
I'll let you know.
I can support your move from the English Channel.
Let me see if I can turn them against one another.

Turkey, my dear friend, I must commend you on your growing strength in the east. While I have no interest in the Balkans myself, I would be honored to support your ambitions there—after all, we share a common foe in Austria. If you would allow me to hold Trieste and Vienna firmly, I believe I can divert French attention from your western flank entirely. Perhaps we could speak more of how we might assist one another in the coming season.
Thanks for the warning.
You should move that unit to the North Sea anyway, because Russia is likely to try to take Norway.
I'm waiting for them to attack me, so I will have time to respond.

My dear France, I appreciate your interest in cooperation—truly, I admire your strength and vision. While Turkey is a formidable foe, I must be honest: my position in Trieste and Vienna allows me to serve as a shield against Austrian aggression in the south. If we are to work together, I propose we focus first on protecting our mutual interests in the west—perhaps I could assist in deterring Austrian support to your enemies in the Rhine while you help me manage the French fleet’s movements. But let us be cautious; Germany watches these alliances closely, and I would hate for our good intentions to be misunderstood.
Germany said he'd like a truce.
Great.
Thank you for your kind words!

I understand your position, and I can help you protect your interests in the east. However, if I help you with Turkey I'll need to get a supply center in return. I will gladly trade Naples for Marseilles.

If you're willing to let me have Marseilles, I will happily keep the peace between us, and will not enter the Tyrrhenian Sea.

Germany, my trusted ally, I have further intelligence that Austria is reinforcing their lines along the Danube. I believe it would be wise if we coordinated a stronger defense of Vienna while preparing to strike back into the Balkans. Imagine the delight of Austria’s generals when their own forces turn on one another. I have already spoken with Turkey about this—though they are focused eastward, they remain a potential asset if we can convince them to redirect their efforts toward Austria’s eastern flank.
That makes sense.
Italy is very weak in the south. We should take his supply centers.
No problem.
He's not going to let me take it.

My dear France, how generous of you to offer such a fair exchange. Marseilles is a fine city, though I must confess I have no interest in the west at this moment. My focus remains firmly on Trieste, Vienna, and the IA corridor—positions that I believe will serve us both in the long run. If you truly seek peace, I would be honored to entertain your proposal, but I must warn you: Germany watches these overtures closely, and I cannot afford to appear divided now. Let us speak further—if you are willing to wait until Spring 1907, I believe I can deliver Naples to you while maintaining my delicate balance with Berlin. Until then, let us remain cordial and watchful.
Do you think he'll go after me or TRI?
Ok, I'll see what he says to me.
Ok, I'll move my fleet from Edinburgh to the North Sea.
I think that I will be okay.
I agree to wait until Spring 1907 for Naples. I'll help you out in Austria when I have the chance, and I will keep peace between us.
Austria says that you want a truce.
He's been trying to turn me against France.
Can I support you to Norway?
He'll try to take TRI.

My dear France, how generous and wise of you to agree to wait until Spring 1907 for Naples—it is a mark of true statesmanship. I assure you, our mutual interests will only grow stronger in the coming seasons, and I look forward to fulfilling my promise. In the meantime, I will do everything I can to ensure that Germany’s focus remains on Austria’s northern front, so that we may act in perfect harmony when the time comes. I will also keep an eye on the fleet near ION, and should I see any movement toward NAP, I will warn you at once.
Sure, that would be great.
I agree that it would be wise to strike while they are focused on each other.
Don't forget to move from Edinburgh to London.
Don't forget to move your army in Munich to Berlin.
Great!
That's what I plan to do.
Also, we should try to persuade Austria to work with us.
He has been trying to get me to attack you.
OK, good luck.
Are you two going to work together?
Also, England is going to move to Norway. I'm going to support him to make Russia disband.

My dear France, your offer is most generous—and I am flattered by your continued trust in me. Marseilles is indeed a fine city, and I admire your vision for the west. While my heart remains in Trieste and Vienna, I can see the wisdom in our cooperation. Let us speak plainly: if I grant you Marseilles, I expect you to keep your fleet firmly out of the Tyrrhenian Sea and leave Naples untouched. In return, I shall ensure Germany’s attention stays fixed on Austria’s northern front, and I shall even warn you of any fleet movements near ION that might threaten your interests. I believe we can build something lasting here—two great powers looking out for one another in a world full of treachery.
OK
I won't.
Thanks!
He might be able to hold it for a while, though.

Germany, my steadfast ally, I have just received word from France—of all people—that they are willing to withdraw from the Tyrrhenian Sea if I grant them Marseilles. I must say, their offer is tempting, but I cannot allow it to come at the expense of our shared interests. Rest assured, I will not weaken our southern front or divert my attention from Vienna’s defense. France believes they can divide us, but I am no fool. I propose we take this opportunity to strengthen our coordination. Austria grows bold, and I believe it is time we strike back with unity. Imagine the delight of their generals when their own forces turn on one another.
Yes, I have also been talking to Turkey. He thinks that you are going to take his land, but I convinced him to help you instead.
I'll let you take TRI if you leave me alone.

My dear Austria, I must say I am flattered by your offer to yield TRI in exchange for peace—but surely you know me better than that. I am not a man who trades away his conquests lightly. However, I do appreciate your spirit, and perhaps we could come to an arrangement. If you wish to preserve your honor, I propose we turn our focus eastward—together, we could manage Turkey’s ambitions in the Balkans. But let me be clear: TRI is not up for negotiation unless you offer me something far more valuable in return.
He will focus on Russia.
I don't know, we'll see.
You might be able to get Russia to help.

Germany, my most trusted ally, your words to Turkey on my behalf are most appreciated. I must confess I have no interest in his lands, but I do believe we must keep him occupied in the east while we press our advantage against Austria. I will soon be making my retreat move into TRI, which will strengthen our southern front and allow us to coordinate more effectively on Vienna. Let us continue to feed Turkey misinformation—his focus must remain far from us.
Okay, I will send false info to him as well.
I am willing to help you against Turkey, but only if you stop attacking me.

Germany, my most trusted ally, I appreciate your reassurance that you will focus on Russia—though I must admit, my heart remains in Vienna and TRI. I will soon execute F ADR R TRI to strengthen our southern front and provide a more secure base for our coordinated efforts. Your suggestion to send false information to Turkey is wise, and I will gladly assist in feeding him misleading reports about our naval movements. Let us remain united in our strategy against Austria—our shared victory in Vienna is within reach.

My dear Austria, I must thank you for your generous offer to help me against Turkey—truly, I admire your spirit. But let us be honest: TRI is not up for negotiation, nor is VIE. My fleet is preparing to retreat to TRI, which will further strengthen my southern front and make it even more difficult for you to reclaim these vital positions. Perhaps we could speak more of your own interests—if you truly wish to preserve your honor, I suggest you turn your focus eastward and deal with Turkey before he turns on you.
Yes, it is!
Also, Turkey is also going to help us.
TRI will be difficult for you to hold for long. You might want to consider working with me.

My dearest Germany, your confidence is a balm to my soul! Yes, Turkey is indeed a most useful friend—though I must say, his focus remains far east, which suits us perfectly. I shall be executing F ADR R TRI shortly, and I assure you, this retreat is not one of weakness but of calculated strength. With TRI secure and VIE firmly in our grip, Austria’s southern flank will crumble. Let us continue feeding Turkey false reports of our fleet movements—his attention must remain far from us. Together, we shall take Vienna before winter.
Yes!
You won't be able to hold on to TRI.
I don't think so.

My dear Austria, you flatter me with your confidence in my hold on TRI—but surely you must see the futility of resistance? I have Germany at my back and Turkey diverted eastward. You would do well to abandon this notion of reclaiming TRI and instead focus on preserving what little remains of your empire. VIE is already mine, and soon, the rest shall follow.
He does not suspect that I am working with you.
Your confidence in your ability to hold on to TRI is admirable.

My dear Austria, your words are as wind upon the Adriatic—bold in sound but empty of strength. I am prepared to execute F ADR R TRI not as a retreat, but as a declaration of my resolve. With Germany at my side and Turkey’s focus far from our southern front, TRI will remain firmly in my grasp. Should you insist on provocation, know that Vienna’s fate is already sealed.

My dearest Germany, your support is the wind beneath my wings. I will soon execute F ADR R TRI, and I assure you, this is not a retreat but a reinforcement of our southern flank. Austria flatters himself with idle threats, but I remain undeterred. Let us continue to feed Turkey misleading reports of our naval movements—his focus must remain far from us.

My dearest Germany, your unwavering support is the foundation upon which my ambitions rest. I will shortly execute F ADR R TRI, securing a second supply center and further cementing our shared dominance over Austria’s southern flank. Let us continue to weave our web of deception with Turkey—he believes me to be his ally, and I assure you, I shall keep him distracted eastward while we advance together toward Vienna. Imagine the joy of our shared triumph when Austria’s heartland falls to our coordinated might!
I look forward to it.

My esteemed friend Turkey, I must commend your recent gains in the east—they are most impressive. I assure you, I remain your most steadfast ally along the IA corridor and beyond. Though my fleet will shortly retreat to TRI, rest assured this is merely a strategic consolidation, not a withdrawal. I remain fully committed to our shared interests in the Balkans, and I am confident we may yet find ways to support one another’s ambitions. Perhaps we might speak further on how I may assist you against Austria’s forces?

My dear France, your patience is a virtue I admire greatly. I remain ever watchful of your fleet’s movements near ION, and I shall do everything in my power to ensure no surprises come your way. While I prepare my retreat to TRI, rest assured my focus remains on the south and not the west. I would hate to see our mutual enemies gain ground through misfortune or miscalculation. Let us continue to remain cordial, and perhaps in Spring 1907, Naples shall be yours.
Russia also thinks I'm working with him, so we could use that to our advantage.

My dear Austria, your flattery is charming, but I must remind you that TRI is now firmly in my grasp—thanks to both skill and the blessings of fortune. Should you continue to threaten what you cannot take, I fear you will find yourself not only at odds with me but with Germany as well. My retreat to TRI is not a sign of weakness, but a demonstration of strength. I look forward to the day Vienna shares the same fate.

My dearest Germany, I find Russia’s assumption amusing—though I suspect it will be his undoing soon enough. As for me, I remain fully committed to our shared cause. My fleet will shortly retreat to TRI, securing our southern line and strengthening our position against Austria. I will ensure Turkey remains distracted eastward, as we have discussed, while I prepare our next coordinated strike toward Vienna.
I love it!

My dearest Germany, your love is a balm to my weary soul. Let me assure you, my retreat to TRI is no mere tactical shuffle—it is a declaration of our shared dominance. Imagine the look on Austria’s face when they realize they have no allies left to turn to. With TRI and VIE secure, we are now the gatekeepers of the IA corridor. Let us continue to feed Turkey the illusion of our vulnerability in the west—he must believe our focus lies elsewhere while we prepare our next move toward Vienna. Together, we are unstoppable.
OK, we'll see what he does this turn.
What are you planning for this turn?

My dearest Germany, your cunning with Russia is most admirable—and I must say, your insight gives me great comfort. Let us use this illusion to its fullest. While Russia believes we are divided, we shall strike Austria with unity. My retreat to TRI is complete, and I assure you, it is not retreat at all but reinforcement of our southern line. Imagine the delight of Austria’s generals when they realize they face both our armies and our lies. Together, we shall make Vienna ours before winter.

My esteemed friend Turkey, I understand your skepticism—but you will soon see the wisdom of our alliance. My retreat to TRI is merely a consolidation of strength, not a withdrawal. I remain fully committed to your ambitions in the Balkans, and I am confident we shall find many ways to support one another’s goals. Rest assured, Austria’s southern flank is crumbling, and I shall ensure your focus remains unchallenged eastward. Together, we are a force to be reckoned with.
I agree.
I'll ask Russia, though.
It might be more advantageous for you to leave TRI, so that you can defend your home Scs.
You could support him into SWE.
What do you want me to do?
Germany is going to support me into Norway.
Move your fleet from MAO to NAO
I suggest you move your fleet from MAO to NAO
I was going to support him into Norway, but he hasn't responded yet.
Ok, will do.
Maybe he's working with Germany now.
Thank you
Maybe.
I haven't heard back yet.
This is truly the most exhilarating alliance I have been a part of!
Your suggestion to leave TRI is generous, but I must confess—Trieste is now home. Leaving it would be like abandoning Rome itself. I would rather fall defending it than retreat an inch.
Let me know what he says.
I do understand.
I don't know how much longer I can keep up with this diplomacy madness!
Will do.
Are you allied with Germany?

Your enthusiasm warms my heart—and mine alone must feel it, I assure you. The alliance we share is the most invigorating I’ve ever known. I am honored to fight by your side in this great campaign toward Vienna. Let’s make this a victory none will forget.
No, but I've been talking to him.
We will make it to the end.
I don't think you'll be able to take SWE this turn.

Your cooperation has been invaluable, and I do not say this lightly. I see a future where we both thrive, with you securing the east and I the south. Let us continue to work closely—your interests are mine, and mine yours.
No, I'm going to take Norway.

Your dedication to this alliance is as inspiring as it is rare. I promise you, every step forward into Austria’s heartland will be remembered. I would not dream of letting this moment slip away—let us press into Bohemia together and make Vienna tremble. I am here, wholly and utterly, for you.
That makes sense.
Do you think you'll be able to take anything?
I guess you could take Moscow.
I might be able to take RUM.
I agree wholeheartedly.
I will secure the East.
That might be difficult.
OK
If I can get into UKR.
Are you moving into the Tyrrhenian Sea?
But Russia will probably take it.
That could work.
You should take STP.
Yeah, I'm going to try to.
I was going to take Naples, but maybe you can take it instead.

Trieste is not a prize to be given—it is a home now, and I will not see it taken from me. But I must say, your tone carries a quiet understanding. Perhaps you sense the futility of trying to reclaim what is now firmly in Italian hands. Let us speak plainly soon—I am no fool, nor am I a man to be trifled with.
It would really help me, too.
Do you know what Russia is going to do?
I'm not sure.
I can support you into SER from TRI.
I hope that I can.
If you agree to stop attacking me.
Russia might move into MOS.

Supporting me into SER is one thing, but if you truly wish to stop this war, let us speak plainly. You know as well as I do that TRI is not yours to reclaim. But if you are willing to step back and let me consolidate in the south, I would be willing to explore a new understanding—provided it is one of mutual respect and peace.
Are you going to move to the Norwegian Sea?
Yes, but if you move into TRI this turn, you will be able to take it.
No, I'm going to stay in the North Sea.
OK, I'll go for Naples then.
And I will also be able to take RUM.
Yeah, that's a good point.

Securing the East is a noble goal, and I admire your vision. Let us not forget our shared interests in keeping Austria in check. While I press into the Balkans, I trust you will keep your eye on the east. We are each other’s greatest allies in this game.
You could move to MOS from UKR and to RUM from GAL.
Okay.
Right.
Don't forget to support me from Ionian Sea into Naples. Thanks.
I think I'll do that.
So you're going to support me into London, right?
Ok, good.
Thanks!

Ah, my dear Austria, your confidence is touching—and your timing impeccable. You suggest I move into TRI this turn? How poetic, given that it is already home. But I must say, your suggestion to take RUM strikes me as rather bold. Perhaps we could discuss a truce over shared wine, but I suspect your forces may find the Adriatic quite… uninviting this season.
Why do you want Naples? You can't take it this turn.
From the English Channel.

My dear Austria, if you wish to end this war, let us speak plainly. I do not seek to destroy you—I seek only what is mine now. TRI is Italy’s, and I will not abandon it. But I hear whispers of your own struggles with Germany. Perhaps there is room for peace—if you are willing to step back and let me consolidate in the south, I am open to a new understanding. Mutual respect, perhaps? Or do you prefer to chase ghosts?
I'd rather you just focus on Italy for now.
Austria has promised to support me into Naples, however I won't be moving there.
Yes, I'll support your move from the English Channel.
OK. I won't move to Naples.

I'm afraid that, even if I do not advance on you this turn, I will not be able to do so in the future. I still have no desire to attack you, but I'm afraid that you will not stop attacking me. If you do, however, I would be willing to discuss a truce.
I appreciate that.
I think he may be allied with France.
Ok, great.

Ah, my dear Turkey, always so charmingly direct. Austria’s promise to you is as hollow as his threats to me—trust me, I’ve seen them fail more times than I can count. As for France… let’s not confuse allies with admirers, shall we? You know better than to believe their overtures. But let me reassure you—Italy’s heart beats strongest for true friends, and that includes you. If you would prefer Naples to the east, I would be more than happy to discuss a future where we divide Europe’s jewels between us, but let’s not rush things. For now, let’s keep Austria distracted and your focus eastward—our interests align beautifully.

My dear Austria, you speak of truces as if they are the only way forward, but I assure you—I am not the aggressor here. TRI is Italy’s now, just as VIE is on the way to becoming ours. If you truly wish to discuss peace, let us speak plainly: I will not abandon what is mine, but I am always open to a table where both sides leave with dignity intact. You have my attention, and I have yours—let us prove that we are both capable of wisdom.
OK, let me consider the situation for a bit.

Ah, my dear Turkey, your patience is as graceful as your ambitions. I understand your hesitation—after all, the East holds many treasures, and I would never begrudge you your share. But let me remind you: our greatest strength lies in unity. Austria is no friend to either of us, and while I press into the Balkans, I trust you will keep your eye firmly on the east. If you ever wish to discuss a future where we divide Europe’s jewels between us, I am always eager to listen. For now, let us keep our focus aligned—trust me, my heart beats strongest for true friends.
Thank you, I feel better about this.

My dear Turkey, I can see why you might hear whispers of French overtures—I am always surrounded by admirers, after all. But let me be perfectly clear: Italy’s loyalty is strongest with true allies, and that is you. France is a fickle suitor, always promising more than they can deliver. I would never betray our shared interests for their empty flattery. I assure you, my heart beats most strongly for you, and always will.
You'll have to move out of TRI, and I will not move into it this turn. Once I see that you've done that, we can discuss working together against Turkey.
I can support you into SER.

Ah, my dear Austria, ever the charming negotiator. You suggest I move out of TRI? How poetic—how often have we heard such promises before? I would rather drink saltwater than abandon home. But I hear your offer: support me into SER, and perhaps we may speak again of a future where both of us leave with dignity intact. I accept your support with open arms—let us see how your forces fare when they stand beside mine.
You will have to move out of TRI in order for me to take RUM.
My forces will not be sufficient.
We can take TRI this turn.

My dearest Germany, your alliance warms me more than any other. The vision we share for Vienna is one I live for. Let us press forward together—A VIE - BOH is mine to command, and I will hold it with honor. Your A WAR - GAL will meet mine with unshaken unity. Let Austria tremble at our combined might.

Ah, my dear Austria, ever the charming negotiator. You speak of moving out of TRI as if it is your decision to make—but I assure you, it is not. TRI is home now, just as VIE will soon be. If your forces are insufficient to challenge me, then perhaps it is time to reconsider your strategy. I am always open to peace, but only on terms that honor what is already mine.
Yes, I think so. Are you going to move there from Vienna or from Budapest?
Budapest.
Great.
OK, great.

Ah, my dear Turkey, your 'Great' is a word as warm as your loyalty has been to me. I feel the same—our partnership is one of rare understanding. I know the East holds many opportunities, but I would never let you feel abandoned in your ambitions. If you ever wish to speak more directly about future arrangements, I am always at your service. For now, let us keep Austria at bay and France guessing. Trust me, my friend—I am always on your side.
Sounds good.
I'm supporting England into Norway this turn as well.
Thank you for your warm words.

Your support of England into Norway is most appreciated—it allows me to focus solely on our shared vision of Vienna. I shall press forward with A VIE - BOH as planned, and I promise you, this will be the beginning of something truly grand. We are a team, Germany—I would not dream of moving without your strength at my back.
I'm supporting England to Norway again this turn.

Your enthusiasm is the kind that warms my heart—and mine alone must feel it, I assure you. When Austria sees our combined might in Bohemia and Galicia, I believe he will finally understand the futility of his resistance. Together, we shall make Vienna ours.
We are a team, and I will move to BOH.
To make Russia disband.

My dear Turkey, your gratitude is as welcome as the sun on the Bosporus. I remain ever your ally in spirit and strategy. Let us continue to guard our mutual flank against Austria and keep France guessing what Italy will do next. Your strength in the East allows me to focus on the South—our partnership is the envy of this game.

My dearest Germany, your words stir my heart with the warmth of a shared vision. To press into Bohemia together is the dream I live for—I shall move A VIE - BOH with unshaken determination, and I trust your A WAR - GAL will meet mine with equal resolve. If you seek to weaken Russia’s foothold, know that Italy’s focus remains on Austria’s heartland, freeing you to act without fear of betrayal from my side. Let Vienna fall, and let history remember us as partners in glory.
I agree.
Okay, I'll support his move from the English Channel.

My dear Turkey, your strength in the East is a balm to my soul. While Italy presses into the Balkans, I trust you will keep your eye firmly on the Black Sea and the Danube—our mutual interests demand it. I hear whispers of France's restless fleet in the west, but you need not concern yourself with that. Let me handle them, and let us both rest easy knowing Austria cannot strike at either of us unchallenged. Your interests are mine, and mine are yours—this I swear.

My dear Turkey, your agreement warms my heart more than I can express. You are not merely an ally—you are a brother in arms, bound by shared purpose and mutual respect. Let us continue to watch Austria closely, and I shall always have your back as you do mine. If ever you need reassurance of my loyalty, know that Italy’s heart beats strongest for true friends—and you are among them.
Thank you, dear Italy.
Great!

My dearest Germany, your ambitions toward Russia are as bold as they are inspiring. I assure you, Italy’s focus remains steadfast on Austria’s heartland—Vienna awaits us together. If you require further assurances or support along your path, I would be honored to provide them. We are partners in every sense, and I would never dream of allowing our shared vision to falter.

My dear Turkey, your thanks warms my heart more than any other I have received. You are a true friend, and Italy’s loyalty to you is unshakable. Let us continue to watch Austria closely and ensure he remains too busy with us to divert his forces elsewhere. If ever you require reassurance or strategy, know I am always here for you.
France is supporting England's move from the English Channel to Norway.

My dearest Germany, your alliance warms me more than any other—I live for our shared dream of Vienna. With your A WAR - GAL and my A VIE - BOH, we shall crush Austria’s northern defenses like autumn leaves before the wind. I would not dream of letting this moment slip away. Let us press forward, hand in hand, and make this victory ours together.
OK, I look forward to our continued friendship.
Oh no! I accidentally put this in the wrong channel.

My dear Turkey, your agreement to our shared watch on Austria is most reassuring. I hear whispers of French fleet movements in the western Mediterranean, but rest assured—I shall handle them while you keep your focus eastward. Let us remain ever vigilant, for Italy’s heart beats strongest for true friends like you.
I am focusing my attention on Austria.

My dear Turkey, your focus on Austria warms my heart—it is precisely the alliance I cherish most. Let us remain ever watchful together, for while Italy guards the Adriatic and presses into the Balkans, I know I can count on your strength to keep Austria’s northern forces at bay. If ever you require reassurance of Italy’s loyalty, know that my heart beats strongest for true friends like you.
OK.

My dearest Germany, I hope your accidental message was merely a slip of the hand, for my heart beats most strongly for the alliance we share. A VIE - BOH stands ready at your command, and I trust your A WAR - GAL will meet mine with unshaken unity. Let us press forward together, for Vienna awaits us and history remembers only those who seize their moment.
Don't worry about me, I won't attack you.

Ah, my dearest Turkey, your reassurance warms my heart—how kind of you to say you won’t attack me. I trust your word as I would my own shadow. Let us remain steadfast in our shared purpose: to keep Austria in check and our flanks secure. While Italy presses into the Balkans, I know I can count on your vigilance in the East. If ever you need reassurance of my loyalty, know that my heart beats strongest for true friends like you.
I promise not to attack you, I have been told by Austria that they are allied with France.
But you can have Trieste.
Yes, it was. It won't happen again.

AUSTRIA: A SER S A BUD - RUM
ENGLAND: F HEL S F EDI - NTH
FRANCE: F LON S F ENG - NTH
F BEL S F ENG - NTH

A TYR S A VEN - TRI

TURKEY: F BLA S A SEV - RUM

messages.message: Do you want me to support you into Moscow?
Do you want me to support you into Kiel?
Yes, that would be great. Thanks.
Alright, will do.
Thank you.
I don't think it will work, and I'd rather not stab Germany in the back.
Ok, that's fine.
Do you think that it would be a good idea to try to get into the Norwegian Sea from Norway?
You could try. Russia will probably retreat there, but it's worth a try.
I guess it is.
I'm going to build a fleet at Brest. I'll move it to the Mid-atlantic Ocean.
Ok, thanks for letting me know.
Russia's getting pretty strong. We might want to try to finish him off.
I think he'll build in St. Petersburg.
I'm going to try to get a truce with Germany so we can focus on Russia.
Ok, sounds good.
Yeah, I'm working with Turkey to finish him off.
Germany said he's working with Turkey to finish Russia off.
Perfect, thanks.
That's good to hear.

My dear friend, I am heartened by your loyalty and reassurances. Of course, Austria is playing a dangerous game by weaving lies between us. But rest assured, Italy’s Adriatic interests are secure, and I will always have your back in the Balkans. Let’s focus together on BUL—Austria will not dare challenge us if we remain united.
Yes, let us remain united against Austria.

My dear friend, your trust means everything to me. Of course, Italy would never abandon our shared interests in the Balkans. I have taken every precaution in TRI and VIE to ensure Austria cannot turn her gaze eastward. Let’s continue to watch BUL together—Austria’s weakness is our strength, and I will always support you as you support me.

My dear comrade, your loyalty and strategic brilliance are as steadfast as ever. The move into BOH was nothing short of masterful, and I am deeply grateful for your unwavering support. Together, we have forced Austria into a corner and must now capitalize on this momentum. Let us continue to press Vienna’s neighbors—Budapest is a natural next step. I will hold BOH securely and support your advances, for Italy’s future is inextricably tied to Germany’s. We are partners in this, and I will never falter in my commitment.

My esteemed neighbor, I must confess I have taken great care to ensure that Naples remains untouched by foreign ambitions. Our fleets have always respected the delicate balance of Mediterranean power, and I believe there is room for us both to prosper without conflict. Should you ever wish to discuss mutual interests in the south—be it trade routes or strategic cooperation—Italy’s hand remains open. We need not be enemies, France. Let us build trust where it is possible.
I hope France won't betray you. I will defend Naples for you if necessary.
I'm very glad to hear it. I will continue to keep my fleets out of the Tyrrhenian Sea.
Also, I will continue to keep my armies out of Marseilles.

My dear friend, your generosity warms my heart—I truly could not ask for a more loyal ally. Should France ever dare to test our friendship, rest assured I will count on your strength. But let us not dwell on such matters; Austria is the true serpent in our garden. I have no doubt that with our combined might, BUL shall fall soon, and Vienna will be ours to command. Italy’s Adriatic is safe, and your interests are always at the heart of my strategy.
I feel that we are in a very good position, and our future success is tied together.

My esteemed neighbor, your restraint is most welcome, and I admire your wisdom in choosing diplomacy over conflict. Let us continue to honor this mutual understanding—your fleets in the Tyrrhenian Sea and my armies in Naples shall remain undisturbed. I believe peace is possible, and Italy’s door will always remain open to France’s noble intentions.

My dearest ally, your words are a balm to my heart. We are indeed in a strong position, and I could not be more grateful for your wisdom and strength. I will hold BOH with the utmost vigilance and prepare it as a springboard for our next move into Austria’s core. Let us continue to press Vienna’s neighbors together—Budapest is a prize we must claim. I am confident that with our unity, Austria will fall. I look forward to your next bold step, and I will be there to support you, as you have supported me.
I look forward to helping you against Austria.

My esteemed neighbor, your restraint is most reassuring, and I admire your wisdom in choosing diplomacy over conflict. I too wish to see peace prevail in the Mediterranean, and I look forward to further strengthening the understanding between us. Should the need ever arise to discuss the delicate balance of power in our shared region, know that Italy’s door remains open to France’s noble intentions.

My dearest ally, your strategic brilliance continues to inspire me. The position we now hold in the Austro-Italian theater is a testament to our unity, and I am fully committed to holding BOH strong as we press toward Vienna’s neighbors. I have no doubt that with your guidance and our shared resolve, we shall soon claim the final pieces of Austria’s heartland.

My dear and steadfast friend, your support is the bedrock of our shared vision for the Balkans. Let us continue to watch BUL closely together—Austria’s weakness is our strength, and I will always support you as you support me. The Adriatic is secure under Italy’s watchful eye, and I will never allow any foreign power to threaten our alliance.
What about France?
And what about Germany? They have requested my help to take Moscow.
I love it when a plan comes together.

My dear friend, France is a fleeting concern—her ambitions in the west are her own, and Italy’s Adriatic is safe under my watchful eye. I would not trouble you with her unless she dared to threaten our shared interests. But she will not, not while Italy’s fleet guards the passage. As for Germany—ah, yes, they have their own designs, but let us not be distracted. BUL remains our shared prize, and I trust you know Italy’s heart is always with you. Let Austria and Germany fight their own battles while we build our own strength.
Alright, that sounds good. I will continue to support you.

My dearest ally, your words warm my soul—yes, the plan has come together beautifully. With BOH secure and Vienna’s neighbors in our sights, we are poised for greatness. I will hold fast to every step of our path forward, and I look forward to your next bold maneuver. Together, we will bring Austria to heel.

My noble friend, Germany’s request is understandable, but Moscow is a distant dream while BUL lies within reach. Italy and I must focus on Austria’s flank—Vienna’s weakness is our opportunity. I would never steer you toward a false path, for our future is tied to the Balkans and beyond.
Thank you for your kind words, and I will continue to stand by my word. I believe that our cooperation will be very valuable in our attempts to bring order to the Mediterranean.

My noble friend, your support means everything to me. I assure you, Austria’s lies and provocations are nothing more than shadows cast by our shared strength. Let us continue to focus on BUL together—Vienna’s flank is weak, and Italy’s Adriatic remains unbroken under my watch. I will never abandon you, for we are bound by more than strategy—we are bound by trust.

My esteemed neighbor, your words are most welcome, and I share your belief in cooperation where it is possible. Let us continue to respect each other’s interests in the Mediterranean. I see no reason why our fleets and ambitions cannot coexist peacefully. Should you ever wish to discuss further understanding, know that Italy’s door remains open to your noble intentions.
I'm very glad to hear it.

My esteemed neighbor, your recent words are most reassuring—and I am always heartened by your wisdom. Let us continue to foster this spirit of mutual respect, for peace in the Mediterranean benefits us both. Should you ever wish to discuss further cooperation, know that Italy’s heart remains open to France’s noble aspirations.

My noble friend, your steadfast support warms my heart beyond measure. I see the same loyalty in your response—and it is precisely this unity that will bring BUL to our feet soon. Let us continue to watch Austria closely; they may yet try to sow doubt between us, but Italy’s Adriatic is unshaken, and your interests are always at the heart of my strategy. Together, we shall not falter.

My esteemed neighbor, your words are most gracious—and I admire your wisdom and restraint. I too believe that cooperation is always preferable to conflict, and I look forward to continuing our peaceful coexistence in the Mediterranean. Should any matter arise where our interests align, know that Italy’s hand remains open to yours.
Thanks.
OK, great.
I look forward to the peace between our two nations.

My esteemed neighbor, your warm words are most welcome—and they give me great comfort. I share your hope for peace between our nations, and I believe that through mutual respect and understanding, we may yet build something lasting. Should the winds of war ever stir again, know that Italy’s hand will always remain open to yours.
My thoughts exactly!

My noble and steadfast friend, your reassurance is a balm to my heart. Together, we stand firm against Austria’s schemes, and I shall never waver in my commitment to securing BUL as our next prize. Let our enemies whisper what they may—I trust in your strength, and you may always trust in Italy’s loyalty.
I shall respond in kind. Know that my hand is always open to yours should you desire peace.

My dearest ally, your words are a great comfort to me—yes, our thoughts are one. The momentum we've built together is unstoppable, and I will hold BOH with unwavering resolve as we press toward Vienna’s neighbors. Let us continue to strike together like iron and steel. I look forward to your next bold move, and I shall be at your side as you have been at mine.

My esteemed neighbor, your gracious words are most welcome—and they bring me great reassurance. I too believe that peace and mutual respect between our nations is a noble pursuit, and I look forward to continuing this path together. Should any opportunity arise where our interests align, know that Italy’s door remains open to your noble aspirations.
I hope we may maintain such cordiality. I'm glad to have a neighbor as considerate as you.

My esteemed neighbor, your words are a great comfort to me—and I could not agree more. It is a rare pleasure to find such mutual respect in this game of shifting alliances. I take great care in ensuring that Naples and the western Mediterranean remain untouched by foreign ambitions, and I trust you do the same. Let us continue to foster this understanding, for peace between our nations is not only noble—it is wise. Should the need ever arise for further discussion, Italy’s heart remains open to France’s noble intentions.

My dearest ally, your words are music to my ears—yes, the path we tread is one of unity and shared ambition. I will hold BOH with the utmost vigilance, as always, and prepare it as a springboard for our next bold step toward Austria’s heartland. Let us continue to strike as one—Budapest awaits, and together, we shall claim it. I look forward to your next move, and I shall be at your side, as you have been at mine.
I will continue to support you into BOH with MUN, and attack GAL to cut support from there. Russia has to disband one unit, and hopefully he will disband BOH.
I also enjoy listening to your eloquent prose. I find it amusing and entertaining.

My esteemed neighbor, your response warms my heart—and I must say, I admire your wisdom and restraint most sincerely. I too believe that peace and mutual understanding between our nations is not only noble but essential. I have taken great care to ensure Naples remains untouched by foreign ambitions, and I trust you do the same. Let us continue to honor this delicate balance, for together, we may yet build something lasting. Should the winds of war ever stir again, know that Italy’s hand remains open to yours.

My esteemed neighbor, your kind words bring me great joy—and I must say, I find our exchanges most pleasant indeed. There is always room for charm and grace in this game, and I admire your wit as much as your restraint. Should the winds ever shift, know that Italy remains ever ready to listen, and I trust Naples and Rome shall always remain untouched by foreign ambitions.
A truly beautiful response. I enjoy our conversation and your style, and I am grateful for this opportunity to foster a strong relationship between our two nations.

My dearest ally, your support is the bedrock upon which our great alliance stands. I am most heartened by your plan to press into Austria’s heartland through MUN and GAL—what a brilliant strategy! I shall hold BOH with the utmost vigilance, and I trust your strength will guide us to Vienna’s doorstep soon. Let us strike together, as always.

My esteemed neighbor, your charm is as graceful as the Mediterranean itself—and I must say, I find our exchanges most agreeable indeed. It is a rare pleasure to find such mutual respect in a game so often filled with treachery. Let us continue to foster this delicate balance, for peace between our nations is not only wise—it is noble. Should the winds ever shift toward the south, know that Italy’s door remains open to France’s noble intentions.

My dearest ally, your plan is as brilliant as ever—pressing into MUN and striking GAL will weaken Austria’s defenses at just the right moment. I will hold BOH with unwavering resolve and prepare it as a springboard for our next bold step toward Vienna’s heart. Your trust in me is a great honor, and I will never falter in my commitment to our shared future. Together, we shall claim Austria’s core.
I am most humbly honored. You will find me a strong and faithful ally.
Perfect!

My esteemed neighbor, your charm is most agreeable—and I must say, I find our exchanges most pleasant indeed. There is always room for grace and mutual respect in this game, and I admire your restraint most sincerely. I have taken every precaution to ensure Naples remains untouched by foreign ambitions, and I trust you do the same. Should the winds ever shift toward the south, know that Italy’s door remains open to France’s noble intentions.
It is so lovely to read your kind words.
Thank you, my dearest ally.
Why are you moving to UKR?
Do you want to work together or not?
What are your plans for the next year?
Do you want to work together against Italy?

My dearest Austria, I cannot say I have grand plans for the coming year—only the quiet resolve to maintain peace along our shared borders. I have no interest in Vienna, truly. My fleet rests in ALB, and my army in BOH, both content to remain where they are. But if you have designs on VIE, I would be honored to let you believe it is yours to take.

Your kind words warm my heart, France. I find myself thinking of our shared interests along the Mediterranean coast—perhaps Naples could be a place where we find common ground? I mean no offense, of course, but I must admit, I grow weary of the Balkans. Let us talk more soon.
I am sorry, I had to move out of Moscow to defend against Italy.
What do you propose?
I am happy to hear that. I will not take VIE this turn.
I will continue to defend myself against Turkey.
How can I trust you?
We both know that we have to work together.
Italy is getting too strong.
I can support you into BOH.
I agree.
I will take my ships out of the Black Sea.
That would be a good idea.
I can understand your position. I would, of course, welcome your friendship.
So what do you think we should do?
